pairs.en: Good day, and thank you for standing by. Welcome to the MongoDB third-quarter fiscal year 2025 conference call. At this time, all participants are in a listen-only mode. After the speakers' presentation, there'll be a question-and-answer session.
pairs.zh:
大家好，感谢您一直在线等待。欢迎参加MongoDB 2025财年第三季度电话会议。目前，所有参会者均处于只听模式。演讲者发言结束后，将进入问答环节。

Good day, and thank you for standing by. Welcome to the MongoDB third-quarter fiscal year 2025 conference call. At this time, all participants are in a listen-only mode. After the speakers' presentation, there'll be a question-and-answer session.

pairs.en: [Operator instructions] Please be advised that today's conference is being recorded. I would now like to turn the call over to your speaker for today, Brian Denyeau. Please go ahead.
pairs.zh:
[操作员说明]请注意，今天的会议正在录音。现在，有请今天的主讲人Brian Denyeau发言。请开始。

[Operator instructions] Please be advised that today's conference is being recorded. I would now like to turn the call over to your speaker for today, Brian Denyeau. Please go ahead.

pairs.en: Brian Raferty Denyeau--Investor Relations
pairs.zh:
Brian Raferty Denyeau--投资者关系  
Brian Raferty Denyeau--Investor Relations

pairs.en: Thank you, Lisa. Good afternoon, and thank you all for joining us today to review MongoDB's third-quarter fiscal 2025 financial results, which we announced in our press release issued after the close of the market today. Joining me the call today are Dev Ittycheria, president and CEO of MongoDB; and Michael Gordon, MongoDB's COO and CFO. During this call, we will make forward-looking statements, including statements related to our market and future growth opportunities, our expectations for the macroeconomic environment in fiscal 2025 and the impact of AI, the benefits of our product platform, our competitive landscape, customer behaviors, our financial guidance, and our planned investments in growth opportunities in AI.
pairs.zh:
谢谢，Lisa。下午好，感谢大家今天加入我们，回顾MongoDB 2025财年第三季度的财务业绩，这些业绩已在今日市场收盘后发布的新闻稿中公布。今天与我一同参加电话会议的有MongoDB的总裁兼CEO Dev Ittycheria，以及MongoDB的COO兼CFO Michael Gordon。在本次电话会议中，我们将做出前瞻性陈述，包括与我们的市场和未来增长机会相关的陈述、我们对2025财年宏观经济环境及AI影响的预期、我们产品平台的优势、竞争格局、客户行为、我们的财务指引，以及我们在AI增长机会方面的计划投资。

Thank you, Lisa. Good afternoon, and thank you all for joining us today to review MongoDB's third-quarter fiscal 2025 financial results, which we announced in our press release issued after the close of the market today. Joining me the call today are Dev Ittycheria, president and CEO of MongoDB; and Michael Gordon, MongoDB's COO and CFO. During this call, we will make forward-looking statements, including statements related to our market and future growth opportunities, our expectations for the macroeconomic environment in fiscal 2025 and the impact of AI, the benefits of our product platform, our competitive landscape, customer behaviors, our financial guidance, and our planned investments in growth opportunities in AI.

pairs.en: These statements are subject to a variety of risks and uncertainties, including the results of operations and financial condition that cause actual results to differ materially from our expectations. For a discussion of the material risks and uncertainties that could affect our actual results, please refer to the risks described in our quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended July 31st, 2024 that we filed with the SEC on August 30th, 2024. Any forward-looking statements made on this call reflect our views only as of today, and we undertake no obligation to update them except as required by law. Additionally, we will discuss non-GAAP financial measures on this conference call.
pairs.zh:
这些声明面临多种风险和不确定性，包括经营业绩和财务状况可能导致实际结果与我们预期产生重大差异。如需了解可能影响实际结果的重大风险与不确定性，请参阅我们于2024年8月30日向美国证券交易委员会提交的截至2024年7月31日的季度报告Form 10-Q中所描述的风险。本次电话会议中做出的任何前瞻性陈述仅反映我们截至今天的观点，除非法律要求，否则我们没有义务更新这些陈述。此外，我们将在本次电话会议中讨论非GAAP财务指标。

These statements are subject to a variety of risks and uncertainties, including the results of operations and financial condition that cause actual results to differ materially from our expectations. For a discussion of the material risks and uncertainties that could affect our actual results, please refer to the risks described in our quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended July 31st, 2024 that we filed with the SEC on August 30th, 2024. Any forward-looking statements made on this call reflect our views only as of today, and we undertake no obligation to update them except as required by law. Additionally, we will discuss non-GAAP financial measures on this conference call.

pairs.en: Please refer to the tables in our earnings release in the Investor Relations portion of our website for a reconciliation of these measures to the most directly comparable GAAP financial measure. With that, I'd like to turn the call over to Dev. Dev?
pairs.zh:
请参阅我们网站投资者关系部分的财报发布中的表格，以了解这些指标与最直接可比的GAAP财务指标的对账。接下来，我想把电话交给Dev。Dev？

Please refer to the tables in our earnings release in the Investor Relations portion of our website for a reconciliation of these measures to the most directly comparable GAAP financial measure. With that, I'd like to turn the call over to Dev. Dev?

pairs.en: Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev C. Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Thanks, Brian, and thank you to everyone for joining us today. I'm pleased to report that we had a strong quarter of new business and executed well against our large market opportunity. Let's begin by reviewing our third quarter results before giving you a broader company update. We generated revenue of $529 million, a 22% year-over-year increase and above the high end of our guidance.
pairs.zh:
感谢Brian，也感谢各位今天加入我们。我很高兴地报告，我们本季度新业务表现强劲，并在广阔的市场机遇中执行良好。首先回顾第三季度业绩，再向大家介绍公司整体最新情况。我们实现营收5.29亿美元，同比增长22%，高于我们指引的上限。

Thanks, Brian, and thank you to everyone for joining us today. I'm pleased to report that we had a strong quarter of new business and executed well against our large market opportunity. Let's begin by reviewing our third quarter results before giving you a broader company update. We generated revenue of $529 million, a 22% year-over-year increase and above the high end of our guidance.

pairs.en: Atlas revenue grew 26% year-over-year, representing 68% of total revenue. We generated non-GAAP operating income of $101 million for a 19% non-GAAP operating margin and we ended the quarter with over 52,600 customers. Overall, we were pleased with our performance in the third quarter. We had a strong new business quarter and we're happy with our new workload acquisition on Atlas.
pairs.zh:
Atlas收入同比增长26%，占总收入的68%。我们实现了1.01亿美元的非GAAP营业收入，非GAAP营业利润率为19%，本季度结束时客户数超过52,600。总体而言，我们对第三季度的表现感到满意。我们迎来了强劲的新业务季度，并且对Atlas上的新工作负载获取感到高兴。

原文：Atlas revenue grew 26% year-over-year, representing 68% of total revenue. We generated non-GAAP operating income of $101 million for a 19% non-GAAP operating margin and we ended the quarter with over 52,600 customers. Overall, we were pleased with our performance in the third quarter. We had a strong new business quarter and we're happy with our new workload acquisition on Atlas.

pairs.en: Our non-Atlas business significantly exceeded expectations in part because we benefited from a few large multi-year deals as customers continue to value our run anywhere strategy and want to build a deeper longer-term relationship with MongoDB. Atlas consumption was slightly better than expected in a macro-environment that we would characterize as largely consistent with what we saw in the first half of the year. Michael will cover consumption trends in more detail. Retention rates remained strong in Q3, demonstrating the mission criticality of our platform.
pairs.zh:
我们的非Atlas业务大幅超出预期，部分原因是我们从几个大型多年期交易中受益，因为客户持续重视我们的"随处运行"（run anywhere）战略，并希望与MongoDB建立更深入、更长期的合作关系。在宏观环境与我们上半年所见基本一致的情况下，Atlas的消费情况略好于预期。Michael将更详细地介绍消费趋势。第三季度的留存率保持强劲，彰显了我们平台的关键任务属性。

Our non-Atlas business significantly exceeded expectations in part because we benefited from a few large multi-year deals as customers continue to value our run anywhere strategy and want to build a deeper longer-term relationship with MongoDB. Atlas consumption was slightly better than expected in a macro-environment that we would characterize as largely consistent with what we saw in the first half of the year. Michael will cover consumption trends in more detail. Retention rates remained strong in Q3, demonstrating the mission criticality of our platform.

pairs.en: On our Q1 earnings call, we shared with you the three major strategic initiatives that we believe will enable us to maximize our long-term opportunity. I want to give you an update on the progress we're making on those initiatives. First, we are increasing our investment in the enterprise channel since we see the strongest returns in this part of the market. Specifically, we're expanding our strategic account program going to next year, as we see more accounts that will benefit from incremental investment.
pairs.zh:
在我们第一季度的财报电话会议上，我们向大家分享了三个主要的战略举措，我们认为这些举措将帮助我们最大化长期机遇。现在我想向大家汇报这些举措的进展。首先，我们正在增加对企业渠道的投资，因为我们看到这一市场领域的回报最为强劲。具体来说，我们正在将战略客户项目扩展到明年，因为我们看到更多客户将从增量投资中受益。

On our Q1 earnings call, we shared with you the three major strategic initiatives that we believe will enable us to maximize our long-term opportunity. I want to give you an update on the progress we're making on those initiatives. First, we are increasing our investment in the enterprise channel since we see the strongest returns in this part of the market. Specifically, we're expanding our strategic account program going to next year, as we see more accounts that will benefit from incremental investment.

pairs.en: In addition, we're investing time and resources to educate developers in large enterprise accounts and uplevel their MongoDB skills. These organizations have thousands of developers and as we penetrate them more deeply, we encounter developers who have historically only built SQL applications and simply do not know how to use MongoDB to its full potential. In our experience, educating these developers on the benefits of MongoDB drives significant incremental adoption of our platform. To fund these up-market investments, we are reallocating a portion of our mid-market investments.
pairs.zh:
此外，我们正在投入时间和资源，帮助大型企业客户中的开发者进行培训，提升他们的MongoDB技能。这些组织拥有成千上万的开发者，随着我们更深入地渗透，我们发现一些开发者过去只构建过SQL应用，根本不知道如何充分利用MongoDB。根据我们的经验，向这些开发者传授MongoDB的优势，能够显著推动对我们平台的额外采用。为了支持这些高端市场投资，我们正在重新分配一部分中端市场的投入。

In addition, we're investing time and resources to educate developers in large enterprise accounts and uplevel their MongoDB skills. These organizations have thousands of developers and as we penetrate them more deeply, we encounter developers who have historically only built SQL applications and simply do not know how to use MongoDB to its full potential. In our experience, educating these developers on the benefits of MongoDB drives significant incremental adoption of our platform. To fund these up-market investments, we are reallocating a portion of our mid-market investments.

pairs.en: The mid-market remains an attractive opportunity for us, but we believe that prioritizing investment up-market will deliver strong returns in the current environment. We also believe there are additional ways to serve the mid-market more efficiently through our self-serve channel and other scaled technology-enabled sales and customer service motions. Second, we are optimistic about the opportunity to accelerate legacy app modernization using AI and are investing more in this area. As you recall, we ran a few successful pilots earlier this year, demonstrating that AI tooling combined with professional services and our relational migrator product can significantly reduce the time, cost, and risk of migrating legacy applications onto MongoDB.
pairs.zh:
中端市场对我们来说仍然是一个有吸引力的机会，但我们相信，在当前环境下优先投资高端市场将带来强劲回报。同时，我们认为通过自助服务渠道以及其他规模化技术驱动的销售和客户服务方式，可以更高效地服务中端市场。其次，我们对利用AI加速传统应用现代化这一机遇持乐观态度，并正在加大对该领域的投资。如诸位所知，今年早些时候我们成功开展了几项试点项目，证明将AI工具与专业服务及我们的relational migrator产品相结合，能够显著降低将传统应用迁移至MongoDB的时间、成本和风险。

The mid-market remains an attractive opportunity for us, but we believe that prioritizing investment up-market will deliver strong returns in the current environment. We also believe there are additional ways to serve the mid-market more efficiently through our self-serve channel and other scaled technology-enabled sales and customer service motions. Second, we are optimistic about the opportunity to accelerate legacy app modernization using AI and are investing more in this area. As you recall, we ran a few successful pilots earlier this year, demonstrating that AI tooling combined with professional services and our relational migrator product can significantly reduce the time, cost, and risk of migrating legacy applications onto MongoDB.

pairs.en: While it's early days, we have observed a more than 50% reduction in the cost to modernize. On the back of these strong early results, additional customer interest is exceeding our expectations. Large enterprises in every industry and geography are experiencing acute pain from their legacy infrastructure and are eager for more agile, performant, and cost-effective solutions. Not only are customers excited to engage with us, they also want to focus on some of the most important applications in their enterprise, further demonstrating the level of interest and size of the long-term opportunity.
pairs.zh:
虽然目前仍处于早期阶段，但我们观察到现代化成本降低了超过50%。得益于这些强劲的初步成果，客户的额外兴趣超出了我们的预期。各行各业、各个地区的大型企业都因遗留基础设施而面临严重困境，并渴望获得更敏捷、高性能且更具成本效益的解决方案。客户不仅乐于与我们合作，还希望聚焦于企业内一些最重要的应用，这进一步彰显了他们的兴趣程度以及长期机遇的规模。

While it's early days, we have observed a more than 50% reduction in the cost to modernize. On the back of these strong early results, additional customer interest is exceeding our expectations. Large enterprises in every industry and geography are experiencing acute pain from their legacy infrastructure and are eager for more agile, performant, and cost-effective solutions. Not only are customers excited to engage with us, they also want to focus on some of the most important applications in their enterprise, further demonstrating the level of interest and size of the long-term opportunity.

pairs.en: As relational applications encompass a wide variety of database types, programming languages, versions, and other customer-specific variables, we expect modernization projects to continue to include meaningful service engagements in the short and medium term. Consequently, we're increasing our professional services delivery capabilities, both directly and through partners. In the long run, we expect to automate and simplify large parts of the modernization process. To that end, we are leveraging the learnings from early service engagement to develop new tools to accelerate future modernization efforts.
pairs.zh:
由于关系型应用涵盖多种数据库类型、编程语言、版本及其他客户特定变量，我们预计现代化项目在中短期内仍将包含重要的服务参与。因此，我们正在通过直接方式及合作伙伴渠道，增强专业服务交付能力。长期来看，我们期望将现代化过程的很大部分进行自动化和简化。为此，我们正利用早期服务参与中获得的经验，开发新工具以加速未来的现代化工作。

As relational applications encompass a wide variety of database types, programming languages, versions, and other customer-specific variables, we expect modernization projects to continue to include meaningful service engagements in the short and medium term. Consequently, we're increasing our professional services delivery capabilities, both directly and through partners. In the long run, we expect to automate and simplify large parts of the modernization process. To that end, we are leveraging the learnings from early service engagement to develop new tools to accelerate future modernization efforts.

pairs.en: Although it's early days and scaling our legacy app modernization capabilities will take time, we have increased conviction that this motion will significantly add to our growth in the long term. Third, we are investing to capitalize on our inherent technical advantages as a key component of the emerging AI tech stack. As a reminder, MongoDB is uniquely equipped to query rich and complex data structures typical of AI applications. The ability of a database to query rich and complex data structures is crucial because AI applications often rely on highly detailed, interrelated, and nuanced data to make accurate predictions and decisions.
pairs.zh:
尽管现在还为时过早，并且扩展我们的传统应用现代化能力需要时间，但我们更加确信这一举措将从长期来看显著促进我们的增长。第三，我们正在投资利用我们作为新兴AI技术栈关键组成部分的固有技术优势。需要提醒的是，MongoDB在查询AI应用中典型的丰富复杂数据结构方面具有独特优势。数据库查询丰富复杂数据结构的能力至关重要，因为AI应用通常依赖于高度详细、相互关联且微妙的数据来做出准确的预测和决策。

Although it's early days and scaling our legacy app modernization capabilities will take time, we have increased conviction that this motion will significantly add to our growth in the long term. Third, we are investing to capitalize on our inherent technical advantages as a key component of the emerging AI tech stack. As a reminder, MongoDB is uniquely equipped to query rich and complex data structures typical of AI applications. The ability of a database to query rich and complex data structures is crucial because AI applications often rely on highly detailed, interrelated, and nuanced data to make accurate predictions and decisions.

pairs.en: For example, a recommendation system doesn't just analyze a single customer's purchase but also considers their browsing history, peer group behavior, and product categories requiring a database that can query and interlink these complex data structures. In addition, MongoDB's architecture unifies source data, metadata, operational data, and vector data in an all-in-one platform, updating the need for multiple database systems and complex back-end architectures. This enables a more compelling developer experience than any other alternative. From what we see in the AI market today, most customers are still in the experimental stage as they work to understand the effectiveness of the underlying tech stack and build early proof-of-concept applications.
pairs.zh:
例如，推荐系统不仅会分析单个客户的购买行为，还会考虑其浏览历史、同类型用户群体行为以及产品类别，这需要一种能够查询并关联这些复杂数据结构的数据库。此外，MongoDB的架构将源数据、元数据、操作数据和向量数据统一在一个一体化平台上，消除了对多种数据库系统和复杂后端架构的需求。这使得开发者体验比任何其他替代方案都更具吸引力。从目前AI市场的观察来看，大多数客户仍处于实验阶段，他们正在努力理解底层技术栈的有效性，并构建初步的概念验证应用。

For example, a recommendation system doesn't just analyze a single customer's purchase but also considers their browsing history, peer group behavior, and product categories requiring a database that can query and interlink these complex data structures. In addition, MongoDB's architecture unifies source data, metadata, operational data, and vector data in an all-in-one platform, updating the need for multiple database systems and complex back-end architectures. This enables a more compelling developer experience than any other alternative. From what we see in the AI market today, most customers are still in the experimental stage as they work to understand the effectiveness of the underlying tech stack and build early proof-of-concept applications.

pairs.en: However, we are seeing an increasing number of AI apps in production. Today, we have thousands of AI apps on our platform. What we don't yet see is many of these apps actually achieving meaningful product market fit and therefore significant traction. In fact, as you take a step back and look at the entire universe of AI apps, a very small percentage of them have achieved the type of scale that we commonly see with enterprise-specific applications.
pairs.zh:
然而，我们看到越来越多的AI应用正在投入生产。如今，我们的平台上已有数千个AI应用。但我们尚未看到许多应用真正实现有意义的product market fit，从而获得显著traction。事实上，如果你退一步审视整个AI应用领域，只有极小比例达到了企业级应用常见的规模。

原文：
However, we are seeing an increasing number of AI apps in production. Today, we have thousands of AI apps on our platform. What we don't yet see is many of these apps actually achieving meaningful product market fit and therefore significant traction. In fact, as you take a step back and look at the entire universe of AI apps, a very small percentage of them have achieved the type of scale that we commonly see with enterprise-specific applications.

pairs.en: We do have some AI apps that are growing quickly, including one that is already a seven-figure workload that has grown 10 times since the beginning of the year. Similar to prior platform shifts as the usefulness of AI tech improves and becomes more cost-effective, we will see the emergence of many more AI apps that do nail product market fit, but it's difficult to predict when that will happen more broadly. We remain confident that we will capture our fair share of these successful AI applications as we see that our platform is popular with developers building more sophisticated AI use cases. We continue investing in our product capabilities, including enterprise-grade Atlas Vector Search functionality to build on this momentum and even better position MongoDB to capture the AI opportunity.
pairs.zh:
我们确实有一些增长迅速的AI应用，其中一个已经是七位数的工作负载，自年初以来增长了10倍。类似于之前的平台转型，随着AI技术的实用性提升和成本效益提高，我们将看到更多能够真正实现产品市场契合的AI应用出现，但很难预测何时会更大规模地发生。我们仍然相信，我们将从这些成功的AI应用中获取应有的份额，因为我们看到我们的平台受到开发更复杂AI用例的开发者的欢迎。我们继续投资于产品能力，包括企业级的Atlas Vector Search功能，以巩固这一势头，并使MongoDB更好地抓住AI机遇。

原文：
We do have some AI apps that are growing quickly, including one that is already a seven-figure workload that has grown 10 times since the beginning of the year. Similar to prior platform shifts as the usefulness of AI tech improves and becomes more cost-effective, we will see the emergence of many more AI apps that do nail product market fit, but it's difficult to predict when that will happen more broadly. We remain confident that we will capture our fair share of these successful AI applications as we see that our platform is popular with developers building more sophisticated AI use cases. We continue investing in our product capabilities, including enterprise-grade Atlas Vector Search functionality to build on this momentum and even better position MongoDB to capture the AI opportunity.

pairs.en: In addition, as previously announced, we are bringing search and vector service to our community and EA offerings, leveraging our run anywhere competitive advantage in the world of AI. Finally, we are expanding our MongoDB AI applications program or MAAP, which helps enterprise customers build and bring AI applications into production by providing them with reference architectures, integrations with leading tech providers, and coordinated services and support. Last week, we announced a new code of partners including McKinsey, Confluent, Capgemini, and Unstructured, as well as the collaboration with Meta to enable developers to build AI-enriched applications on MongoDB using Llama. Next, I'd like to provide you with a brief product update.
pairs.zh:
此外，如之前所宣布，我们正将搜索与向量服务引入社区版和EA版产品，利用我们在AI领域“随处运行”的竞争优势。最后，我们正在扩展MongoDB AI应用计划（MAAP），该计划通过提供参考架构、与领先技术提供商的集成以及协调服务与支持，帮助企业客户构建并将其AI应用投入生产。上周，我们宣布了新的合作伙伴计划，包括McKinsey、Confluent、Capgemini和Unstructured，以及与Meta的合作，使开发者能够使用Llama在MongoDB上构建AI增强型应用。接下来，我将简要介绍产品更新。

In addition, as previously announced, we are bringing search and vector service to our community and EA offerings, leveraging our run anywhere competitive advantage in the world of AI. Finally, we are expanding our MongoDB AI applications program or MAAP, which helps enterprise customers build and bring AI applications into production by providing them with reference architectures, integrations with leading tech providers, and coordinated services and support. Last week, we announced a new code of partners including McKinsey, Confluent, Capgemini, and Unstructured, as well as the collaboration with Meta to enable developers to build AI-enriched applications on MongoDB using Llama. Next, I'd like to provide you with a brief product update.

pairs.en: At our dot local developer conference in London in October, we announced the general availability of MongoDB 8.0, the fastest and most performant version of MongoDB ever. MongoDB 8.0 performs 20% to 60% better against common industry benchmarks compared to our prior version and is built to exceed our customers' most stringent security, resiliency, availability, and performance requirements. To best serve our customers, we regularly review and reprioritize investments in our product portfolio to ensure we're allocating our resources to products with the highest demand from our customers. And to do that, we also deprecate products that are not showing results we desired.
pairs.zh:
在今年10月于伦敦举办的dot local开发者大会上，我们宣布了MongoDB 8.0正式全面可用，这是MongoDB有史以来最快、性能最强的版本。与上一版本相比，MongoDB 8.0在常见行业基准测试中性能提升20%至60%，旨在满足客户最严苛的安全、弹性、可用性和性能要求。为了更好地服务客户，我们定期审查并重新调整产品组合的投资优先级，确保将资源分配给客户需求最高的产品。与此同时，我们也会弃用那些未达预期效果的产品。

At our dot local developer conference in London in October, we announced the general availability of MongoDB 8.0, the fastest and most performant version of MongoDB ever. MongoDB 8.0 performs 20% to 60% better against common industry benchmarks compared to our prior version and is built to exceed our customers' most stringent security, resiliency, availability, and performance requirements. To best serve our customers, we regularly review and reprioritize investments in our product portfolio to ensure we're allocating our resources to products with the highest demand from our customers. And to do that, we also deprecate products that are not showing results we desired.

pairs.en: Consequently, we made the decision to consolidate our Atlas serverless offerings with our smallest dedicated tiers to create Atlas Flex customers, a new offering with a simpler architecture that provides the elasticity features akin to serverless. We will begin migrating effective customers to the single, simple, entry-level solution in Q4. We also decided to deprecate Atlas DeviceSync and other capabilities not widely adopted in order to focus our engineering resources on the core platform. While these reprioritization decisions are not made lightly, they allow us to deliver the most value to the largest number of customers, reinforcing our commitment to being the best modern database and helping us to grow faster.
pairs.zh:
因此，我们决定将Atlas serverless产品与我们最小的专用层级合并，创建Atlas Flex客户——一种架构更简单、提供类似serverless弹性特性的新方案。我们将在Q4开始将现有客户迁移至这一单一、简单的入门级解决方案。同时，我们决定弃用Atlas DeviceSync及其他未被广泛采用的功能，以便将工程资源集中在核心平台上。尽管这些优先级的调整并非轻率之举，但它们使我们能够为最大数量的客户创造最大价值，进一步强化我们成为最佳现代数据库的承诺，并帮助我们实现更快的增长。

原文：
Consequently, we made the decision to consolidate our Atlas serverless offerings with our smallest dedicated tiers to create Atlas Flex customers, a new offering with a simpler architecture that provides the elasticity features akin to serverless. We will begin migrating effective customers to the single, simple, entry-level solution in Q4. We also decided to deprecate Atlas DeviceSync and other capabilities not widely adopted in order to focus our engineering resources on the core platform. While these reprioritization decisions are not made lightly, they allow us to deliver the most value to the largest number of customers, reinforcing our commitment to being the best modern database and helping us to grow faster.

pairs.en: Now, I'd like to spend a few minutes reviewing the adoption trends of MongoDB across our customer base. Customers across industries and around the world are running mission-critical projects in MongoDB Atlas, leveraging the full power of our developer data platform, including Financial Times, CarGurus, and Victoria's Secret. As part of the digital transformation journey, global specialty retailer Victoria's Secret & Company migrated its e-commerce platform to MongoDB Atlas. As a fully managed platform, MongoDB Atlas allowed the company to simplify its architecture and improve performance, supporting the retailer to provide a resilient, secure, and fast web and mobile e-commerce experience for their millions of customers around the world.
pairs.zh:
现在，我想花几分钟回顾一下MongoDB在我们客户群体中的采用趋势。来自全球各行各业的客户都在MongoDB Atlas上运行关键任务项目，充分利用我们开发者数据平台的全部功能，其中包括Financial Times、CarGurus和Victoria's Secret。作为数字化转型的一部分，全球专业零售商Victoria's Secret & Company将其电商平台迁移到了MongoDB Atlas。作为一个全托管平台，MongoDB Atlas使该公司得以简化架构并提升性能，帮助该零售商为全球数百万客户提供弹性、安全且快速的网页及移动端电商体验。

Now, I'd like to spend a few minutes reviewing the adoption trends of MongoDB across our customer base. Customers across industries and around the world are running mission-critical projects in MongoDB Atlas, leveraging the full power of our developer data platform, including Financial Times, CarGurus, and Victoria's Secret. As part of the digital transformation journey, global specialty retailer Victoria's Secret & Company migrated its e-commerce platform to MongoDB Atlas. As a fully managed platform, MongoDB Atlas allowed the company to simplify its architecture and improve performance, supporting the retailer to provide a resilient, secure, and fast web and mobile e-commerce experience for their millions of customers around the world.

pairs.en: Allianz, Alphamad, Swiss Post, and Paylocity are turning to MongoDB to modernize applications. Paylocity, a leading provider of cloud-based payroll and human capital management software, selected MongoDB to power proprietary application aimed at fostering employee connections and engagement. When traffic increased and the original SQL-based solution was unable to keep up with the required performance metrics, Paylocity migrated to MongoDB Atlas to take advantage of the flexible schema architecture, performance, and scalability. MongoDB costs five times less than the previous SQL database solution and the company's developers can now create an application within minutes, something that used to take weeks.
pairs.zh:
Allianz, Alphamad, Swiss Post和Paylocity正在转向MongoDB以实现应用现代化。Paylocity是一家领先的基于云的薪资和人力资本管理软件提供商，选择了MongoDB来驱动旨在促进员工联系和参与度的专有应用。当流量增加且原先基于SQL的解决方案无法满足所需性能指标时，Paylocity迁移到了MongoDB Atlas，以利用其灵活的架构、性能和可扩展性。MongoDB的成本是之前SQL数据库解决方案的五分之一，而且该公司的开发人员现在可以在几分钟内创建一个应用，而过去这需要数周时间。

原文：
Allianz, Alphamad, Swiss Post, and Paylocity are turning to MongoDB to modernize applications. Paylocity, a leading provider of cloud-based payroll and human capital management software, selected MongoDB to power proprietary application aimed at fostering employee connections and engagement. When traffic increased and the original SQL-based solution was unable to keep up with the required performance metrics, Paylocity migrated to MongoDB Atlas to take advantage of the flexible schema architecture, performance, and scalability. MongoDB costs five times less than the previous SQL database solution and the company's developers can now create an application within minutes, something that used to take weeks.

pairs.en: Mature companies and start-ups alike are using MongoDB to help deliver the next wave of AI-powered application to customers, including NerdWallet, Cisco, and Tealbook. Tealbook, a supplier intelligence platform, migrated from Postgres, pgvector, and ElasticSearch to MongoDB to eliminate technical debt and consolidate their tech stack. The company experienced workload isolation and scalability issues in pgvector and were concerned with the search index inconsistencies, which were all resolved with the migration to MongoDB. With Atlas Vector search and dedicated search nodes, Tealbook has realized improved cost-efficiency and increased scalability for the supplier data platform, an application that uses GenAI to collect, verify, and enrich supplier data across various sources.
pairs.zh:
成熟的初创公司与大型企业都在使用MongoDB向客户交付新一代AI驱动的应用，包括NerdWallet、Cisco和Tealbook。供应商智能平台Tealbook从Postgres、pgvector和ElasticSearch迁移至MongoDB，以消除技术债务并整合技术栈。该公司在pgvector中遇到了工作负载隔离和可扩展性问题，并对搜索索引不一致感到担忧，这些问题均通过迁移至MongoDB得到解决。借助Atlas Vector搜索和专用搜索节点，Tealbook在供应商数据平台上实现了更高的成本效益和可扩展性，该平台利用GenAI从多个来源收集、验证和丰富供应商数据。

Mature companies and start-ups alike are using MongoDB to help deliver the next wave of AI-powered application to customers, including NerdWallet, Cisco, and Tealbook. Tealbook, a supplier intelligence platform, migrated from Postgres, pgvector, and ElasticSearch to MongoDB to eliminate technical debt and consolidate their tech stack. The company experienced workload isolation and scalability issues in pgvector and were concerned with the search index inconsistencies, which were all resolved with the migration to MongoDB. With Atlas Vector search and dedicated search nodes, Tealbook has realized improved cost-efficiency and increased scalability for the supplier data platform, an application that uses GenAI to collect, verify, and enrich supplier data across various sources.

pairs.en: In summary, we had a healthy Q3 with both Atlas and EA exceeding expectations. We saw a strong new business quarter and we remain confident in our ability to become an increasing strategic provider in our large and growing market. Looking forward, we see a great opportunity to grow our adoption in the enterprise through new workloads, modernizing legacy applications, and winning the next generation of AI-powered applications. I would like to finish by providing an update on our senior leadership.
pairs.zh:
总括而言，我们第三季度表现稳健，Atlas和EA均超出预期。新业务季度势头强劲，我们仍对自己在庞大且不断增长的市场中成为日益重要的战略供应商的能力充满信心。展望未来，我们看到了通过新工作负载、现代化改造传统应用以及赢得下一代AI驱动应用来扩大企业采用率的巨大机遇。最后，我想汇报一下我们高级领导团队的最新情况。

原文：
In summary, we had a healthy Q3 with both Atlas and EA exceeding expectations. We saw a strong new business quarter and we remain confident in our ability to become an increasing strategic provider in our large and growing market. Looking forward, we see a great opportunity to grow our adoption in the enterprise through new workloads, modernizing legacy applications, and winning the next generation of AI-powered applications. I would like to finish by providing an update on our senior leadership.

pairs.en: First, as we announced early in the press release, after nearly 10 years, Michael Gordon has made the decision to leave MongoDB. Michael has been instrumental in MongoDB's success over the past decade, leading our successful IPO, helping us grow our revenue nearly 50-fold, and scaling -- and successfully scaling our business model to generate meaningful operating leverage. He has been a trusted advisor and business partner to the Board and me over the years and also has become a personal friend. Michael is excited to take a well-deserved break.
pairs.zh:
首先，正如我们在新闻稿早些时候宣布的，在将近10年后，Michael Gordon决定离开MongoDB。过去十年间，Michael对MongoDB的成功至关重要：他领导了公司成功的IPO，帮助我们将营收增长近50倍，并成功扩展了业务模式以产生显著运营杠杆。多年来，他一直是董事会和我本人信赖的顾问与业务伙伴，也成为了我的私人朋友。Michael很期待享受一段应得的假期。

原文：
First, as we announced early in the press release, after nearly 10 years, Michael Gordon has made the decision to leave MongoDB. Michael has been instrumental in MongoDB's success over the past decade, leading our successful IPO, helping us grow our revenue nearly 50-fold, and scaling -- and successfully scaling our business model to generate meaningful operating leverage. He has been a trusted advisor and business partner to the Board and me over the years and also has become a personal friend. Michael is excited to take a well-deserved break.

pairs.en: We have commenced the search for Michael's replacement and will be evaluating both internal and external candidates. One of Michael's proudest compliments -- accomplishments has been building a world-class finance team under his leadership, and I'm confident that we will not miss a beat during this transition. Michael will continue to serve as CFO through January 31st to help us finish the fiscal year and then will transition to an Advisor to the company to ensure a seamless process. If we have not named Michael's successor by fiscal year-end, Serge Tanjga, SVP of Finance, will serve as Interim CFO, beginning on February 1st.
pairs.zh:
我们已开始寻找Michael的继任者，将从内部和外部候选人中进行评估。Michael最值得骄傲的成就之一，是在他的领导下打造了一支世界级的财务团队，我相信这次过渡不会出现任何差池。Michael将担任首席财务官至1月31日，以帮助我们完成本财年工作，随后将转任公司顾问以确保流程平稳衔接。若在财年结束前未能确定Michael的继任者，财务高级副总裁Serge Tanjga将从2月1日起担任临时首席财务官。

We have commenced the search for Michael's replacement and will be evaluating both internal and external candidates. One of Michael's proudest compliments -- accomplishments has been building a world-class finance team under his leadership, and I'm confident that we will not miss a beat during this transition. Michael will continue to serve as CFO through January 31st to help us finish the fiscal year and then will transition to an Advisor to the company to ensure a seamless process. If we have not named Michael's successor by fiscal year-end, Serge Tanjga, SVP of Finance, will serve as Interim CFO, beginning on February 1st.

pairs.en: Second, we are promoting Cedric Pech, currently our chief revenue officer to the newly created role of president worldwide field operations. In this new position, Cedric will oversee all our field-based customer-facing and go-to-market enablement teams, including professional services. We believe this org structure will best enable us to execute on some of the key strategic initiatives I discussed earlier, in particular, our increased focus on up-market and the app monetization opportunity. I would like to congratulate Cedric on this well-deserved promotion.
pairs.zh:
第二，我们将提拔现任首席营收官Cedric Pech担任新设立的全球现场运营总裁一职。在新职位上，Cedric将负责所有面向客户的现场团队以及市场进入赋能团队，包括专业服务。我们相信这种组织架构将最有助于执行我之前讨论的一些关键战略举措，特别是我们加大对高端市场和应用变现机会的投入。我想祝贺Cedric获得这次当之无愧的晋升。

Second, we are promoting Cedric Pech, currently our chief revenue officer to the newly created role of president worldwide field operations. In this new position, Cedric will oversee all our field-based customer-facing and go-to-market enablement teams, including professional services. We believe this org structure will best enable us to execute on some of the key strategic initiatives I discussed earlier, in particular, our increased focus on up-market and the app monetization opportunity. I would like to congratulate Cedric on this well-deserved promotion.

pairs.en: With that, let me turn the call over to Michael.
pairs.zh:
那么，我把电话转给Michael。

With that, let me turn the call over to Michael.

pairs.en: Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer
pairs.zh:
迈克尔·戈登（Michael Gordon）——首席运营官兼首席财务官

Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer

pairs.en: Thanks, Dev, and thanks for the kind words and our incredible partnership over the past decade. The past 10 years have been the most rewarding in my professional career and I'm extremely proud of what we've achieved together and of course, to the whole MongoDB team. With as much success as we had, I still believe that MongoDB is in the early stages of realizing its full potential as it continues to take share in one of the largest markets in software. Now, turning to the results for the quarter.
pairs.zh:
谢谢Dev，也谢谢你的美言，以及过去十年我们不可思议的合作关系。过去的十年是我职业生涯中最有成就感的十年，我为我们一起取得的成就感到无比自豪，当然也要感谢整个MongoDB团队。尽管我们已经取得了如此大的成功，但我仍然相信MongoDB正处于实现其全部潜力的早期阶段，因为它继续在软件行业中最大的市场之一中占据份额。现在，来看本季度的业绩。

Thanks, Dev, and thanks for the kind words and our incredible partnership over the past decade. The past 10 years have been the most rewarding in my professional career and I'm extremely proud of what we've achieved together and of course, to the whole MongoDB team. With as much success as we had, I still believe that MongoDB is in the early stages of realizing its full potential as it continues to take share in one of the largest markets in software. Now, turning to the results for the quarter.

pairs.en: I'll begin with a detailed review of our third-quarter results and then finish with our outlook for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025. First, I'll start with our third quarter results. Total revenue in the quarter was $529.4 million, up 22% year-over-year and above the high end of our guidance. Shifting to our product mix, Atlas grew 26% in the quarter compared to the previous year and now represents 68% of total revenue compared to 66% in the third quarter of fiscal 2024 and 71% last quarter.
pairs.zh:
我将首先详细回顾第三季度业绩，然后总结对第四季度及2025整个财年的展望。首先，我从第三季度业绩开始。本季度总收入为$529.4 million，同比增长22%，高于我们指引的上限。转到产品组合，Atlas本季度同比增长26%，目前占总收入的68%，而2024财年第三季度为66%，上一季度为71%。

I'll begin with a detailed review of our third-quarter results and then finish with our outlook for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025. First, I'll start with our third quarter results. Total revenue in the quarter was $529.4 million, up 22% year-over-year and above the high end of our guidance. Shifting to our product mix, Atlas grew 26% in the quarter compared to the previous year and now represents 68% of total revenue compared to 66% in the third quarter of fiscal 2024 and 71% last quarter.

pairs.en: We recognized Atlas revenue primarily based on customer consumption of our platform and that consumption is closely tied to end user activity of their applications. Let me provide some context on Atlas consumption in the quarter. In Q3, consumption was slightly ahead of our expectations. This year's Q3 seasonal improvement was more muted than in years past as expected.
pairs.zh:
我们确认Atlas的收入主要基于客户对我们平台的消费，而该消费与其应用的终端用户活动密切相关。下面我来介绍一下本季度Atlas的消费情况。第三季度，消费略高于我们的预期。与往年一样，今年第三季度的季节性改善幅度比预期更为平缓。

We recognized Atlas revenue primarily based on customer consumption of our platform and that consumption is closely tied to end user activity of their applications. Let me provide some context on Atlas consumption in the quarter. In Q3, consumption was slightly ahead of our expectations. This year's Q3 seasonal improvement was more muted than in years past as expected.

pairs.en: On a year-over-year basis, consumption growth remains below that of prior year period. Turning to non-Atlas revenue. Non-Atlas came in significantly ahead of our expectations. As Dev mentioned, EA new business was strong, and we continue to have success selling incremental workloads into our existing customer base.
pairs.zh:
按年计算，消费增长仍低于去年同期水平。接下来看非Atlas收入。非Atlas收入显著超出我们的预期。正如Dev所提到的，EA新业务表现强劲，我们在现有客户群中持续成功销售增量工作负载。

原文：
On a year-over-year basis, consumption growth remains below that of prior year period. Turning to non-Atlas revenue. Non-Atlas came in significantly ahead of our expectations. As Dev mentioned, EA new business was strong, and we continue to have success selling incremental workloads into our existing customer base.

pairs.en: In addition, our Q3 non-Atlas revenue benefited from a few large multi-year deals. As you know, due to ASC 606, we recognized the entire term license component of a multi-year contract at the start of that contract. Compared to Q3 of last year, the multi-year license component of non-Atlas revenues was over $15 million higher. Turning to customer growth.
pairs.zh:
此外，我们第三季度的non-Atlas收入得益于几个大额多年期交易。如你们所知，由于ASC 606，我们在多年期合同开始时确认了该合同的整个定期许可部分。与去年第三季度相比，non-Atlas收入中的多年期许可部分增加了超过1500万美元。转向客户增长。

In addition, our Q3 non-Atlas revenue benefited from a few large multi-year deals. As you know, due to ASC 606, we recognized the entire term license component of a multi-year contract at the start of that contract. Compared to Q3 of last year, the multi-year license component of non-Atlas revenues was over $15 million higher. Turning to customer growth.

pairs.en: During the third quarter, we grew our customer base by approximately 1,900 customers sequentially, bringing our total customer count to over 52,600, which is up from over 46,400 in the year-ago period. Of our total customer count, over 7,400 are direct sales customers, which compares to over 6,900 in the year-ago period. The growth in our total customer count is being driven primarily by Atlas, which had over 51,100 customers at the end of the quarter compared to over 44,900 in the year-ago period. It is important to keep in mind that the growth in our Atlas customer count reflects new customers to MongoDB in addition to existing EA customers adding their first Atlas workload.
pairs.zh:
第三季度，我们的客户数量环比增长了约1,900个，使总客户数超过52,600个，而去年同期为超过46,400个。总客户中，超过7,400个为直销客户，而去年同期为超过6,900个。总客户数的增长主要由Atlas驱动，该季度末Atlas拥有超过51,100个客户，而去年同期为超过44,900个。需要留意的是，Atlas客户数的增长既反映了MongoDB的新客户，也反映了现有EA客户首次使用Atlas工作负载的情况。

During the third quarter, we grew our customer base by approximately 1,900 customers sequentially, bringing our total customer count to over 52,600, which is up from over 46,400 in the year-ago period. Of our total customer count, over 7,400 are direct sales customers, which compares to over 6,900 in the year-ago period. The growth in our total customer count is being driven primarily by Atlas, which had over 51,100 customers at the end of the quarter compared to over 44,900 in the year-ago period. It is important to keep in mind that the growth in our Atlas customer count reflects new customers to MongoDB in addition to existing EA customers adding their first Atlas workload.

pairs.en: Continuing on, in Q3, our net ARR expansion rate was approximately 120%. We ended the quarter with 2,314 customers with at least $100,000 in ARR and annualized MRR, up from $1,972 in the year-ago period. Moving down the income statement, I'll be discussing our results on a non-GAAP basis unless otherwise noted. Gross profit in the third quarter was $405.7 million, representing a gross margin of 77%, which is flat versus the year-ago period.
pairs.zh:
继续，第三季度，我们的净ARR扩张率约为120%。季度末，我们拥有2,314个至少拥有10万美元ARR和年化MRR的客户，高于去年同期的1,972个。接下来看利润表，除非另有说明，我将以非GAAP为基础讨论我们的业绩。第三季度毛利润为4.057亿美元，毛利率为77%，与去年同期持平。

Continuing on, in Q3, our net ARR expansion rate was approximately 120%. We ended the quarter with 2,314 customers with at least $100,000 in ARR and annualized MRR, up from $1,972 in the year-ago period. Moving down the income statement, I'll be discussing our results on a non-GAAP basis unless otherwise noted. Gross profit in the third quarter was $405.7 million, representing a gross margin of 77%, which is flat versus the year-ago period.

pairs.en: Our income from operations was $101.5 million or 19% operating margin for the third quarter compared to an 18% operating margin in the year-ago period. The primary reason for more favorable operating income results versus guidance is our revenue outperformance, including the very high margin multi-year license revenue benefit. Net income in the third quarter was $98.1 million or $1.16 per share, based on 84.2 million diluted weighted average shares outstanding. This compares to a net income of $79.1 million or $0.96 per share on 83.7 million diluted weighted-average shares outstanding in the year-ago period.
pairs.zh:
我们的运营收入为1.015亿美元，第三季度运营利润率为19%，而去年同期运营利润率为18%。运营业绩优于预期的主要原因是我们的收入表现超出预期，包括高利润率的多年期许可收入收益。第三季度净利润为9810万美元，每股收益1.16美元，基于8420万股稀释加权平均流通股。相比之下，去年同期净利润为7910万美元，每股收益0.96美元，基于8370万股稀释加权平均流通股。

Our income from operations was $101.5 million or 19% operating margin for the third quarter compared to an 18% operating margin in the year-ago period. The primary reason for more favorable operating income results versus guidance is our revenue outperformance, including the very high margin multi-year license revenue benefit. Net income in the third quarter was $98.1 million or $1.16 per share, based on 84.2 million diluted weighted average shares outstanding. This compares to a net income of $79.1 million or $0.96 per share on 83.7 million diluted weighted-average shares outstanding in the year-ago period.

pairs.en: Turning to the balance sheet and cash flow, we ended the third quarter with $2.3 billion in cash, cash equivalents, short-term investments, and restricted cash. Operating cash flow in the third quarter was $37.4 million. After taking into consideration approximately $2.9 million in capital expenditures and principal repayments of finance lease liabilities, free cash flow was $34.6 million in the quarter. This compares to free cash flow of $35 million in the year-ago period.
pairs.zh:
转向资产负债表和现金流，截至第三季度末，我们持有现金、现金等价物、短期投资及受限资金共计23亿美元。第三季度运营现金流为3740万美元。扣除约290万美元的资本支出及融资租赁负债本金偿还后，本季度自由现金流为3460万美元。去年同期自由现金流为3500万美元。

Turning to the balance sheet and cash flow, we ended the third quarter with $2.3 billion in cash, cash equivalents, short-term investments, and restricted cash. Operating cash flow in the third quarter was $37.4 million. After taking into consideration approximately $2.9 million in capital expenditures and principal repayments of finance lease liabilities, free cash flow was $34.6 million in the quarter. This compares to free cash flow of $35 million in the year-ago period.

pairs.en: In Q3, we did not incur capital expenditures to purchase IPV4 addresses as we previously expected, but we did start making those purchases in November and still expect a total outlay of $20 million to $25 million this fiscal year as we previously communicated. I'd now like to turn to our outlook for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025. For the fourth quarter, we expect revenue to be in the range of $515 million to $519 million. We expect non-GAAP income from operations to be in the range of $55 million to $58 million and non-GAAP net income per share to be in the range of $0.62 to $0.65 based on 84.9 million estimated diluted weighted-average shares outstanding.
pairs.zh:
第三季度，我们并未如先前预期的那样产生购买IPv4地址的资本支出，但我们在11月确实开始进行这些采购，并仍预计本财年的总支出如先前所述为2000万至2500万美元。接下来，我想谈谈我们第四季度及整个2025财年的展望。第四季度，我们预计营收将在5.15亿至5.19亿美元之间。我们预计非GAAP运营收入在5500万至5800万美元之间，基于预计稀释加权平均流通股数8490万股，非GAAP每股净利润在0.62美元至0.65美元之间。

In Q3, we did not incur capital expenditures to purchase IPV4 addresses as we previously expected, but we did start making those purchases in November and still expect a total outlay of $20 million to $25 million this fiscal year as we previously communicated. I'd now like to turn to our outlook for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025. For the fourth quarter, we expect revenue to be in the range of $515 million to $519 million. We expect non-GAAP income from operations to be in the range of $55 million to $58 million and non-GAAP net income per share to be in the range of $0.62 to $0.65 based on 84.9 million estimated diluted weighted-average shares outstanding.

pairs.en: For the full-fiscal year 2025, we expect revenue to be in the range of $1.973 billion to $1.977 billion, non-GAAP income from operations to be in the range of $242 million to $245 million, and non-GAAP net income per share to be in the range of $3.01 to $3.03 based on 84 million estimated diluted weighted-average shares outstanding. Note that the non-GAAP net income per share guidance for the fourth-quarter and full-fiscal year 2025 includes a non-GAAP tax provision of approximately 20%. I'll now provide some more context around our updated guidance. First, in terms of Atlas consumption, we expect to see a typical seasonal slowdown in Q4, driven by underlying application usage moderating during the holiday season.
pairs.zh:
对于2025整个财年，我们预计营收在19.73亿至19.77亿美元之间，非GAAP运营利润在2.42亿至2.45亿美元之间，基于约8,400万股的预计稀释加权平均流通股，非GAAP每股净利润在3.01至3.03美元之间。请注意，2025年第四季度和整个财年的非GAAP每股净利润指引中包含了约20%的非GAAP税项拨备。接下来，我将对我们更新的指引提供更多背景信息。首先，关于Atlas的消费量，我们预计第四季度会出现典型的季节性放缓，原因是假期期间底层应用使用量趋于减少。

For the full-fiscal year 2025, we expect revenue to be in the range of $1.973 billion to $1.977 billion, non-GAAP income from operations to be in the range of $242 million to $245 million, and non-GAAP net income per share to be in the range of $3.01 to $3.03 based on 84 million estimated diluted weighted-average shares outstanding. Note that the non-GAAP net income per share guidance for the fourth-quarter and full-fiscal year 2025 includes a non-GAAP tax provision of approximately 20%. I'll now provide some more context around our updated guidance. First, in terms of Atlas consumption, we expect to see a typical seasonal slowdown in Q4, driven by underlying application usage moderating during the holiday season.

pairs.en: Second, since Atlas consumption remained lower on a year-over-year basis in Q3, we expect to see continued deceleration of Atlas year-over-year growth in Q4. Third, we expect to see a sequential decline in non-Atlas revenue in Q4, which is contrary to our normal pattern. The reason for this is that we experienced a significant additional benefit from multi-year deals in Q3, which we do not expect to recur in Q4. In addition, I want to provide some incremental color on some of our recent product and -- how some of our recent product and go-to-market changes will impact the growth of our reported customer count going forward.
pairs.zh:
其次，由于Atlas消费量在第三季度同比仍处于较低水平，我们预计第四季度Atlas的同比增速将继续放缓。第三，我们预计第四季度非Atlas收入将环比下降，这与我们的正常模式相反。原因是我们在第三季度从多年期合同中获得了显著的额外收益，而这一收益预计不会在第四季度重现。此外，我想补充说明一下我们近期的一些产品以及市场推广策略的变化将如何影响未来报告客户数量的增长。

Second, since Atlas consumption remained lower on a year-over-year basis in Q3, we expect to see continued deceleration of Atlas year-over-year growth in Q4. Third, we expect to see a sequential decline in non-Atlas revenue in Q4, which is contrary to our normal pattern. The reason for this is that we experienced a significant additional benefit from multi-year deals in Q3, which we do not expect to recur in Q4. In addition, I want to provide some incremental color on some of our recent product and -- how some of our recent product and go-to-market changes will impact the growth of our reported customer count going forward.

pairs.en: First, as Dev explained, we are reallocating a portion of our go-to-market resources from the mid-market to the enterprise channel. As a result, we expect to see significantly fewer mid-market direct sales customer net additions and as a result, slower direct sales customer growth going forward. We believe this reallocation of investment dollars will drive higher revenue growth over time. So, it's a trade-off that makes sense.
pairs.zh:
首先，正如Dev所解释的，我们正将一部分市场推广资源从中端市场重新分配至企业渠道。因此，我们预计中端市场直销客户净增数将显著减少，未来直销客户增长也将放缓。我们相信，这种投资资金的重新分配将最终推动更高的营收增长。因此，这是一个合理的权衡。

First, as Dev explained, we are reallocating a portion of our go-to-market resources from the mid-market to the enterprise channel. As a result, we expect to see significantly fewer mid-market direct sales customer net additions and as a result, slower direct sales customer growth going forward. We believe this reallocation of investment dollars will drive higher revenue growth over time. So, it's a trade-off that makes sense.

pairs.en: Second, as we introduce Atlas Flex clusters in Q4 and automatically migrate customers in Q1, we expect to see a one-time negative impact to our customer count since we have approximately 4,000 serverless customers who are very low spending and we do not expect them to transition over to Flex. These customers have a negligible impact on our revenue but will impact our reported customer count. To summarize, we're pleased with our third quarter results and especially our ability to win new business. We have a small share in one of the largest and fastest-growing markets in all of software with a number of secular tailwinds, including AI at our back.
pairs.zh:
第二，随着我们在第四季度推出Atlas Flex集群，并在第一季度自动迁移客户，我们预计客户数量将受到一次性负面影响，因为我们约有4,000名Serverless客户，其支出非常低，我们认为他们不会过渡到Flex。这些客户对我们的收入影响微乎其微，但会影响我们报告的客户数量。总结来说，我们对第三季度的业绩感到满意，尤其是我们赢得新业务的能力。我们在所有软件领域中最大且增长最快的市场之一占据较小份额，同时拥有包括人工智能在内的多项长期利好因素。

Second, as we introduce Atlas Flex clusters in Q4 and automatically migrate customers in Q1, we expect to see a one-time negative impact to our customer count since we have approximately 4,000 serverless customers who are very low spending and we do not expect them to transition over to Flex. These customers have a negligible impact on our revenue but will impact our reported customer count. To summarize, we're pleased with our third quarter results and especially our ability to win new business. We have a small share in one of the largest and fastest-growing markets in all of software with a number of secular tailwinds, including AI at our back.

pairs.en: We'll continue investing judiciously and focusing on our execution to capture this long-term opportunity. With that, we'd like to open it up to questions. Operator?
pairs.zh:
我们将继续保持审慎投资，专注于执行以把握这一长期机遇。接下来，我们开放提问环节。话务员？

原文：We'll continue investing judiciously and focusing on our execution to capture this long-term opportunity. With that, we'd like to open it up to questions. Operator?

pairs.en: Yeah. So, just to talk about agents, I think when you think about agents, there's jobs, there's -- sorry, there's a job, there's projects and then there's task. Yeah. So, just to talk about agents, I think when you think about agents, there's jobs, there's -- sorry, there's a job, there's projects and then there's task. Right now, the agents that are being rolled out are really focused on task like say something from Sierra or some other companies are rolling out agents. But you're right, what they deemed to do is to deal with being able to create a rich and complex data structures.

pairs.en: Now why is this important for AI is that AI models don't just look at isolated data points, but they need to understand relationships, hierarchies, and patterns within the data. They need to be able to essentially get real-time insights. For example, if you have a chatbot where someone is querying, a customer is kind of trying to get some update on the order they placed five minutes ago because they may have not gotten any confirmation, your chatbot needs to be able to deal with real-time information. You need to be able to deal with basically handling very advanced use cases, understanding like, to do things like fraud detection to understand behaviors of supply chains, you need to understand intricate data relationships.
pairs.zh:
现在，这对AI为什么重要？因为AI模型不仅需要查看孤立的数据点，还需要理解数据内部的关系、层次结构和模式。它们必须能够实质上获得实时洞察。例如，如果你有一个聊天机器人，当有人查询时，比如一位客户正试图获取他们五分钟前所下订单的更新信息，因为他们可能没有收到任何确认，那么你的聊天机器人就需要能处理实时信息。你需要能够处理非常高级的用例，比如理解欺诈检测、理解供应链行为等任务，这就需要理解复杂的数据关系。

Now why is this important for AI is that AI models don't just look at isolated data points, but they need to understand relationships, hierarchies, and patterns within the data. They need to be able to essentially get real-time insights. For example, if you have a chatbot where someone is querying, a customer is kind of trying to get some update on the order they placed five minutes ago because they may have not gotten any confirmation, your chatbot needs to be able to deal with real-time information. You need to be able to deal with basically handling very advanced use cases, understanding like, to do things like fraud detection to understand behaviors of supply chains, you need to understand intricate data relationships.

pairs.en: All these things are consistent with MongoDB offers. And so we believe that at the end of the day, we are well-positioned to handle this. And the other thing that I would say is that we've embedded in a very natural way of search and vector search. So we're just not an OLTP database, we do tech search and vector search.
pairs.zh:
所有这些都与MongoDB提供的功能一致。因此我们相信，归根结底，我们完全有能力应对这一点。另外我想说的是，我们以非常自然的方式嵌入了搜索和向量搜索。所以，我们不仅仅是一个OLTP数据库，我们还做技术搜索和向量搜索。

All these things are consistent with MongoDB offers. And so we believe that at the end of the day, we are well-positioned to handle this. And the other thing that I would say is that we've embedded in a very natural way of search and vector search. So we're just not an OLTP database, we do tech search and vector search.

So, we did make some changes at the beginning of the year, and we really wanted to focus on both the volume and the quality of the workloads and there were some slight adjustments that we made. We think those changes are having a reasonable positive impact.

pairs.en: Again, it's too early to declare victory because these workloads usually start small and grow over time, but we're really pleased with the results we're seeing so far. And -- but again, it's early days. And obviously, we'll know about the fiscal '25 workloads as we go into fiscal '26. But so far, so good.
pairs.zh:
再次宣布胜利还为时过早，因为这些工作负载通常从小规模开始，随着时间推移逐渐增长，但我们对目前看到的结果非常满意。而且——不过，这仍处于早期阶段。显然，随着我们进入2026财年，我们将会了解2025财年的工作负载情况。但到目前为止，一切顺利。

Again, it's too early to declare victory because these workloads usually start small and grow over time, but we're really pleased with the results we're seeing so far. And -- but again, it's early days. And obviously, we'll know about the fiscal '25 workloads as we go into fiscal '26. But so far, so good.

There's always been a multiyear component and we continue to see that.

Yeah, thank you. So overall, we continue to find the EA product resonate with customers. It's an important part of the run anywhere strategy and we've continued to see success with that and people wanting to increase their investment in MongoDB. There's always been a multiyear component and we continue to see that.

pairs.en: We talked about that at the beginning of the year as to how fiscal '24 had an abnormally high amount of multi-year benefit and therefore, we were anticipating that being a headwind and we quantified that in roughly the $40 million range. What we talked about on this call earlier is we saw from a few large accounts, a surprising amount of multiyear that positively benefited Q3 at a little more than $15 million in revenue compared to what we saw Q3 a year ago. So, not as much of a headwind as we had been expecting. Obviously, with the 606 dynamics, some of these things, especially for a large deal can be kind of meaty and chunky and lumpy, which is why we try and call it out and sort of help people understand, but there's a pretty healthy kind of baseline flow, not just of EA, but also of multiyear.
pairs.zh:
我们在年初讨论过，24财年有多异常高的多年期收益，因此我们预期这将成为逆风，并量化其影响大约在4000万美元左右。在这次电话会议中我们早些时候提到，从几个大客户那里，我们看到出乎意料的多年期收益在第三季度带来了超过1500万美元的正面收入影响，相比去年第三季度。因此，逆风并没有我们预期的那么大。显然，由于606会计动态，其中一些因素，尤其是大额交易，可能会显得庞大且不均衡，这就是为什么我们试图指出并帮助大家理解，但存在相当健康的基准流量，不仅是EA，还有多年期业务。

pairs.en: Yeah. I just want to add, Tyler, that we are investing in our what we call our EA business. First, we're starting by investing with search and vector search and a community product. That does a couple of things for us.
pairs.zh:
是的。我只是想补充一点，Tyler，我们正在投资于我们所谓的EA业务。首先，我们开始投资于search和vector search以及一个社区产品。这为我们带来了几方面的好处。

Yeah. I just want to add, Tyler, that we are investing in our what we call our EA business. First, we're starting by investing with search and vector search and a community product. That does a couple of things for us.

pairs.en: One, whenever anyone starts with MongoDB with the open-source product, they immediately get all the benefits of that complete highly integrated platform. Two, those capabilities will then migrate to EA. So, EA for us is an investment strategy. We definitely see lots of large customers who are very, very committed to running workloads on-prem.
pairs.zh:
第一，当任何人开始使用开源产品MongoDB时，他们立即获得那个完整且高度集成平台的所有好处。第二，这些能力随后将迁移到EA。所以，对我们来说，EA是一种投资策略。我们确实看到很多大型客户非常、非常致力于在本地运行工作负载。

One, whenever anyone starts with MongoDB with the open-source product, they immediately get all the benefits of that complete highly integrated platform. Two, those capabilities will then migrate to EA. So, EA for us is an investment strategy. We definitely see lots of large customers who are very, very committed to running workloads on-prem.

pairs.en: We even see some customers want to run to run AI workloads on-prem. So, the optionality they get by using MongoDB to not just be on-prem and the cloud, but also cross-cloud is a very compelling one.
pairs.zh:
我们甚至看到一些客户希望在本地运行AI工作负载。因此，他们通过使用MongoDB获得的灵活性不仅局限于本地和云端，还包括跨云的能力，这是非常吸引人的。

We even see some customers want to run to run AI workloads on-prem. So, the optionality they get by using MongoDB to not just be on-prem and the cloud, but also cross-cloud is a very compelling one.

And we're quite energized by those investments and that's something that we have high conviction on.

I think it's again early days. I would say -- I would probably say more on the side of Atlas and EA in the early days. I think once we introduce a search and vector search into the EA product, you'll see more of that on-prem. Obviously, people can use MongoDB for AI workloads using other technologies as well in conjunction with MongoDB for on-prem AI use cases.

pairs.en: But I would say you're probably going to see that happen first in Atlas.

但我想说，你可能首先会在Atlas看到这种情况发生。

But I would say you're probably going to see that happen first in Atlas.

Yeah, thank you. I'm not going to belabor congratulating Michael, but it has been it has been fun working with you and best of luck. The question I had is on the strength in EA. Do you think, Dev, it represents a comment on how enterprises might be rethinking or reassessing the kind of on-prem versus cloud workload placement decision?

pairs.en: Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev C. Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Well, when I think about large enterprises, I think large enterprises have meaningful workloads that are still running on-prem. I think the belief that everything would go to the cloud was probably something that was really popular in the good old days of Zurp. But I think now as customers assess their investments that they already have in place, they're being much more judicious about where they run those workloads and if they think they can leverage their existing investments in their own infrastructure, then they're going to do so. Also for a bunch of other reasons like regulatory reasons, some customers are quite not moving as aggressively to the cloud.
pairs.zh:
嗯，谈到大型企业时，我认为它们仍有大量重要的工作负载运行在本地。过去那种“一切都会迁移到云端”的看法，或许在Zurp的美好旧时光里确实很流行。但现在，随着客户评估已有的投资，它们对工作负载的运行地点变得更加审慎——如果认为可以利用现有基础设施的投资，它们就会这么做。此外，出于监管等一系列其他原因，一些客户并不那么积极地向云迁移。

Well, when I think about large enterprises, I think large enterprises have meaningful workloads that are still running on-prem. I think the belief that everything would go to the cloud was probably something that was really popular in the good old days of Zurp. But I think now as customers assess their investments that they already have in place, they're being much more judicious about where they run those workloads and if they think they can leverage their existing investments in their own infrastructure, then they're going to do so. Also for a bunch of other reasons like regulatory reasons, some customers are quite not moving as aggressively to the cloud.

pairs.en: We see that in particularly in Europe, where we see a lot of the European banks still running majority of the workloads on-prem. So it also varies by region where conversely in Asia, we're seeing people much move much more aggressively to the cloud. So I think it really depends on industry, on geography, and on the personal dynamics of what's happening in that particular account. I mean, we see some large US banks are also very committed to running things on-prem.
pairs.zh:
我们看到，尤其是在欧洲，许多欧洲银行仍将大部分工作负载部署在本地（on-prem）。因此，这也因地区而异——相反在亚洲，我们看到人们的云迁移步伐更为激进。所以我认为这确实取决于行业、地区，以及具体客户账户中正在发生的个人动态。我的意思是，我们看到一些美国大型银行也非常坚持在本地运行业务。

We see that in particularly in Europe, where we see a lot of the European banks still running majority of the workloads on-prem. So it also varies by region where conversely in Asia, we're seeing people much move much more aggressively to the cloud. So I think it really depends on industry, on geography, and on the personal dynamics of what's happening in that particular account. I mean, we see some large US banks are also very committed to running things on-prem.

pairs.en: So it really varies. And that's why we feel really good about our run anywhere strategy because it gives customer optionality. They can build something and run on-prem. And if and when they choose to move to the cloud, it's very easy to do so with MongoDB.
pairs.zh:
所以这确实各不相同。这就是为什么我们对我们的run anywhere策略感到非常满意，因为它为客户提供了可选择性。他们可以构建一些东西并在本地运行。如果他们选择迁移到云端，使用MongoDB也非常容易。

So it really varies. And that's why we feel really good about our run anywhere strategy because it gives customer optionality. They can build something and run on-prem. And if and when they choose to move to the cloud, it's very easy to do so with MongoDB.

Any additional detail would be helpful. Thanks.

Is it hiring a lot of new salespeople? Is it working more with SIs, investing more in SIs? Any additional detail would be helpful. Thanks.

pairs.en: Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer
pairs.zh:
迈克尔·戈登（Michael Gordon）——首席运营官兼首席财务官

Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. So just for everyone's benefit, we've identified a number of accounts, which we call strategic accounts, which we think that have high upside for us. We've seen a number of accounts that grow very quickly when we deploy the right mix of resources. Now they're all not necessarily quota-carrying resources.
pairs.zh:
是的。为了大家的利益，我们已经确定了一些我们称之为战略账户的账户，我们认为这些账户对我们有很高的上升潜力。我们观察到，当配置合适的资源组合时，许多账户会增长得非常快。不过，这些账户不一定都是带有配额的资源。

Yeah. So just for everyone's benefit, we've identified a number of accounts, which we call strategic accounts, which we think that have high upside for us. We've seen a number of accounts that grow very quickly when we deploy the right mix of resources. Now they're all not necessarily quota-carrying resources.

pairs.en: They could be additional technical sales resources, additional PS resources, additional customer service resources to better service and support those accounts. We even do things like run education sessions for developers of the accounts, they're called either hackathons or like what we call developer days or even design reviews where we'll meet with our development teams who are looking to build an application, help them think about how they would potentially use MongoDB to build that particular app. And what we find is that because many of these developers, the experience with MongoDB is quite limited, the more we can engage with them, the more we can educate them and the more we can show them how simple and easy it is. Like for example, most customers today think like they have to use an OLTP database, then a search database, maybe a vector database, and then like a caching database.
pairs.zh:
他们可能会增加技术支持销售资源、专业服务资源及客户服务资源，以更好地服务和支持这些客户。我们甚至会为客户的开发者举办教育会议，称为黑客松或开发者日，甚至进行设计评审，与希望构建应用的开发团队会面，帮助他们思考如何利用MongoDB构建特定应用。我们发现在很多开发者对MongoDB经验有限的情况下，越是与他们互动，越能教育他们，并展示其简单易用性。比如，如今大多数客户认为必须使用OLTP数据库、搜索数据库、向量数据库以及缓存数据库。

They could be additional technical sales resources, additional PS resources, additional customer service resources to better service and support those accounts. We even do things like run education sessions for developers of the accounts, they're called either hackathons or like what we call developer days or even design reviews where we'll meet with our development teams who are looking to build an application, help them think about how they would potentially use MongoDB to build that particular app. And what we find is that because many of these developers, the experience with MongoDB is quite limited, the more we can engage with them, the more we can educate them and the more we can show them how simple and easy it is. Like for example, most customers today think like they have to use an OLTP database, then a search database, maybe a vector database, and then like a caching database.

pairs.en: And all that is integrated in MongoDB. So all of a sudden, customers can say, wow, I can simplify my life, simplify my back-end infrastructure, build this app far more quickly and it will be much more easier to manage long-term if I do everything on MongoDB. And it's really a function of just educating them on the power of MongoDB that really opens up a lot of opportunities for us. So that's why we're doubling down, and the mix of resources is really predicated on the accounts, but it's not just quota counting resources, it's the whole suite of resources that we're bringing to the table.
pairs.zh:
所有这些都集成在MongoDB中。于是客户们突然发现，哇，我可以简化生活、简化后端基础设施，更快地构建这个应用，而且如果我在MongoDB上完成所有操作，长期管理也会更加容易。真正的作用在于向他们介绍MongoDB的强大功能，这为我们打开了许多机会。这就是为什么我们加倍投入，资源的分配确实取决于账户，但这不仅仅是配额计算资源，而是我们带来的整套资源。

And all that is integrated in MongoDB. So all of a sudden, customers can say, wow, I can simplify my life, simplify my back-end infrastructure, build this app far more quickly and it will be much more easier to manage long-term if I do everything on MongoDB. And it's really a function of just educating them on the power of MongoDB that really opens up a lot of opportunities for us. So that's why we're doubling down, and the mix of resources is really predicated on the accounts, but it's not just quota counting resources, it's the whole suite of resources that we're bringing to the table.

pairs.en: Okay. Good afternoon. Thank you very much for taking the question and congrats on a nice quarter. You gave an example of a customer that migrated off Postgres and I think you said they had issues with their PG vector function.

好的。下午好。非常感谢您回答这个问题，并祝贺您们取得了一个不错的季度。您举了一个客户从Postgres迁移的例子，我想您提到他们的PG vector函数遇到了问题。

原文：Okay. Good afternoon. Thank you very much for taking the question and congrats on a nice quarter. You gave an example of a customer that migrated off Postgres and I think you said they had issues with their PG vector function.

pairs.en: I was wondering how long was that customer using Postgres before they decided to make a change to Mongo, meaning was this some sort of like a rebound type customer where they chose PG -- or excuse me, Postgres and it didn't work? And then how frequently are you seeing this type of transition?
pairs.zh:
我想知道那个客户在使用Postgres多久之后决定改用Mongo？也就是说，这是不是那种所谓的“反弹型”客户——他们先选了PG（哦，抱歉，是Postgres）但没成功？然后，你看到这种转换的频率有多高？

原文：I was wondering how long was that customer using Postgres before they decided to make a change to Mongo, meaning was this some sort of like a rebound type customer where they chose PG -- or excuse me, Postgres and it didn't work? And then how frequently are you seeing this type of transition?

pairs.en: Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev C. Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. So I can't give you the specifics on how long they were using Postgres, but this is not -- this is a trend that we're seeing in our business. You have to remember Postgres is a 40-year-old technology. It's -- and they have been the beneficiary of people lifting and shifting from other types of relational databases, Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL, et cetera.
pairs.zh:
是的，我无法给你具体说明他们使用Postgres的时间长度，但这并不是特例——这是我们业务中正在出现的一种趋势。你要知道Postgres是一项已有40年历史的技术。它——他们一直受益于其他关系型数据库（如Oracle、SQL Server、MySQL等）的迁移。

原文：
Yeah. So I can't give you the specifics on how long they were using Postgres, but this is not -- this is a trend that we're seeing in our business. You have to remember Postgres is a 40-year-old technology. It's -- and they have been the beneficiary of people lifting and shifting from other types of relational databases, Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL, et cetera.

pairs.en: And they're an open-source database. And but as part -- because they're an open-source and relational database, they have the same inherent challenges all relational databases do. They're quite inflexible. So once you build a schema, it's very hard to change the schema.
pairs.zh:
它们是一个开源数据库。但作为一部分——因为它们是开源的关系型数据库，所以它们面临着所有关系型数据库固有的挑战。它们相当不灵活。一旦你构建了一个模式，就很难更改这个模式。

And they're an open-source database. And but as part -- because they're an open-source and relational database, they have the same inherent challenges all relational databases do. They're quite inflexible. So once you build a schema, it's very hard to change the schema.

pairs.en: It's hard to scale and hard to distribute data. And if you have large data volumes, you have to do weird things like, for example, resort to off-road storage for large data objects, which creates performance bottlenecks. And so again, people default to Postgres if they don't know anything better because all they know is relational and everyone is kind of moving off those other relational platforms. And that's the whole point I was saying earlier.
pairs.zh:
数据难以扩展和分发。如果数据量很大，你就得做一些奇怪的事情，比如对大型数据对象采用非关系型存储，这会造成性能瓶颈。因此，如果人们不了解更好的选择，就会默认使用Postgres，因为他们只知道关系型数据库，而其他人都在逐渐放弃那些其他关系型平台。这就是我之前说过的重点。

原文：
It's hard to scale and hard to distribute data. And if you have large data volumes, you have to do weird things like, for example, resort to off-road storage for large data objects, which creates performance bottlenecks. And so again, people default to Postgres if they don't know anything better because all they know is relational and everyone is kind of moving off those other relational platforms. And that's the whole point I was saying earlier.

pairs.en: Once we educate developers on the flexibility of schema, how easily or horizontally scale the rich query language where you can do aggregations and do sophisticated geospatial indexes, the productivity gains by using the document model and how easy is to organize data, it's -- people are just like, wow, life is so much easier. Now I want to be clear, this is not a zero-sum game. Postgres does not have to fail for us to be successful. It's a big market and we're quite excited about the opportunity, but we do see customers moving off Postgres and coming to MongoDB.
pairs.zh:
一旦我们让开发者了解schema的灵活性，了解如何轻松横向扩展支持聚合和复杂地理空间索引的丰富查询语言，以及使用文档模型带来的生产力提升和数据组织的便捷性，他们就会惊叹——生活变得如此简单。但我要澄清，这并非零和游戏。Postgres不必失败我们才能成功。这是一个巨大的市场，我们对此机遇感到兴奋，但我们确实看到客户从Postgres迁移到MongoDB。

Once we educate developers on the flexibility of schema, how easily or horizontally scale the rich query language where you can do aggregations and do sophisticated geospatial indexes, the productivity gains by using the document model and how easy is to organize data, it's -- people are just like, wow, life is so much easier. Now I want to be clear, this is not a zero-sum game. Postgres does not have to fail for us to be successful. It's a big market and we're quite excited about the opportunity, but we do see customers moving off Postgres and coming to MongoDB.
