pairs.en: Yeah. I'll kind of give you just a rundown of some of the -- I mean, essentially you're asking about the new products, like our -- on search, we introduced a new capability called Atlas search nodes, which where you can asymmetrically scale your search nodes because if you have a search-intensive use case, you don't have to scale all your nodes because they have become quite expensive. And we've seen that this kind of groundbreaking capability really well received. The demand is quite high and because customers like they can tune the configuration to the unique needs of their search requirements.
pairs.zh:
好的。我来简单介绍一下——你问的主要是新产品，比如在搜索方面，我们推出了一项新功能叫Atlas search nodes，它可以让你非对称地扩展搜索节点。因为如果你有搜索密集型用例，就不必扩展所有节点，毕竟那样成本很高。我们看到这项突破性功能非常受欢迎，需求也很高，因为客户可以根据自身搜索需求来调整配置。

原文：
Yeah. I'll kind of give you just a rundown of some of the -- I mean, essentially you're asking about the new products, like our -- on search, we introduced a new capability called Atlas search nodes, which where you can asymmetrically scale your search nodes because if you have a search-intensive use case, you don't have to scale all your nodes because they have become quite expensive. And we've seen that this kind of groundbreaking capability really well received. The demand is quite high and because customers like they can tune the configuration to the unique needs of their search requirements.

pairs.en: One of the world's largest banks is using Atlas Search to provide like a Google-like search experience on payments data for massive corporate customers. So this is a customer-facing application and so performance, and scalability are critical. A leading provider of AI-powered accounting software uses Atlas Search to power its invoice analytics future, which allows end users and finance teams to perform ad-hoc analysis and easily find past-due invoices and voices that contain errors. So that search -- on vector search, it -- again, and it's been our kind of our first full-year since going generally available.
pairs.zh:
全球最大的银行之一正在使用Atlas Search为其大型企业客户提供类似Google的支付数据搜索体验。这是一款面向客户的应用程序，因此性能和可扩展性至关重要。一家领先的人工智能会计软件提供商使用Atlas Search为其发票分析功能提供支持，使最终用户和财务团队能够进行临时分析，并轻松找到逾期发票和包含错误的记录。所以那个搜索——关于向量搜索——再次强调，这是自正式发布以来我们的第一个完整年度。

One of the world's largest banks is using Atlas Search to provide like a Google-like search experience on payments data for massive corporate customers. So this is a customer-facing application and so performance, and scalability are critical. A leading provider of AI-powered accounting software uses Atlas Search to power its invoice analytics future, which allows end users and finance teams to perform ad-hoc analysis and easily find past-due invoices and voices that contain errors. So that search -- on vector search, it -- again, and it's been our kind of our first full-year since going generally available.

pairs.en: And the product uptake has been actually very, very high. In Q3, we released quantization for Atlas Vector Search, which reduces the memory requirements by up to 96%, allowing us to support larger vector workloads with vastly improved price performance. For example, a multinational news organization created a GenAI powered tool designed to help producers and journalists efficiently search, summarize, and verify information from vast and varied data sources. A leading security firm is using Atlas Vector Search to fight AI fraud and a leading global media company replaced elastic search with hybrid search and vector search use case for a user recommendation engine that's built to suggest -- that's built in to suggest articles to end users.
pairs.zh:
实际上，产品的采用率非常高。第三季度，我们发布了Atlas Vector Search的量化功能，可将内存需求降低高达96%，从而支持更大规模的向量工作负载，并大幅提升性价比。例如，一家跨国新闻机构创建了一个基于GenAI的工具，旨在帮助制作人和记者高效搜索、总结并验证来自海量多样化数据源的信息。一家领先的安全公司正在使用Atlas Vector Search打击AI欺诈，而一家全球知名媒体公司则用混合搜索和向量搜索用例取代了Elastic Search，用于构建用户推荐引擎——该引擎内置功能，可向终端用户推荐文章。

原文：
And the product uptake has been actually very, very high. In Q3, we released quantization for Atlas Vector Search, which reduces the memory requirements by up to 96%, allowing us to support larger vector workloads with vastly improved price performance. For example, a multinational news organization created a GenAI powered tool designed to help producers and journalists efficiently search, summarize, and verify information from vast and varied data sources. pairs.en: And so that's super exciting to see as well. We're also seeing a lot of interest in our streaming product, demand is very high. We just rolled it out to another hyperscaler, and customers are commenting on that the use cases of being able to embed stream processing with MongoDB makes our life so much easier. So overall, we're quite pleased with the progress we're making on the new products.
pairs.zh:
看到这些也令人非常兴奋。此外，我们的流媒体产品也吸引了大量兴趣，需求非常旺盛。我们刚刚将其部署到另一家超大规模云服务商，客户反馈表示，能够将流处理与MongoDB集成，让他们的工作变得轻松许多。总体而言，我们对新产品的进展感到非常满意。

And so that's super exciting to see as well. We're also seeing a lot of interest in our streaming product, demand is very high. We just rolled it out to another hyperscaler, and customers are commenting on that the use cases of being able to embed stream processing with MongoDB makes our life so much easier. So overall, we're quite pleased with the progress we're making on the new products.

pairs.en: And as I said before, natively bundling all these capabilities really reduces or eliminates the need for customers to have to bolt on a bunch of different technologies to solve the same problem, saving them a lot of time, money, cost, and risk.
pairs.zh:
正如我之前所说，原生集成所有这些功能确实减少或消除了客户为了解决问题而拼接多种不同技术的需求，从而为他们节省了大量时间、金钱、成本和风险。

And as I said before, natively bundling all these capabilities really reduces or eliminates the need for customers to have to bolt on a bunch of different technologies to solve the same problem, saving them a lot of time, money, cost, and risk.

pairs.zh:

pairs.en: Terrific. And maybe for a quick follow-up for Dev. I think it builds off maybe Tyler's question at the top of the Q&A, but you had cited that some customers are thinking about their workloads more holistically and even looking to run AI workloads on-prem. How much of that do you think is just a function of customers are still trying to figure out how to optimize for latency and cost or is this more a demonstration of we really are in the early phases of the exploratory phase versus going into production? Is there any way to coarse that out or is the two not necessarily connected? Thank you.
pairs.zh:
太棒了。另外，可能想快速追问Dev。我认为这延续了Tyler在问答环节开始时提出的问题，但你提到一些客户正在更整体地考虑他们的工作负载，甚至考虑在本地运行AI工作负载。你认为这在多大程度上只是客户仍在努力优化延迟和成本的结果，还是更多表明我们确实仍处于探索阶段而非生产阶段？有没有办法区分这两者，或者它们不一定相关？谢谢。

原文：
Terrific. And maybe for a quick follow-up for Dev. I think it builds off maybe Tyler's question at the top of the Q&A, but you had cited that some customers are thinking about their workloads more holistically and even looking to run AI workloads on-prem. How much of that do you think is just a function of customers are still trying to figure out how to optimize for latency and cost or is this more a demonstration of we really are in the early phases of the exploratory phase versus going into production? Is there any way to coarse that out or is the two not necessarily connected? Thank you.

pairs.en: Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev C. Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: No, I think it's kind of a little bit of both. I think you have some customers who are very committed to running a big part of the estate on-prem. So by definition, then if they're going to build an AI workload, it has to be run on-prem, which means that they also need access to GPUs and they're doing that. And then other customers are leveraging basically renting GPUs from the cloud providers and building their own AI workloads.
pairs.zh:
不，我认为是两者兼而有之。我认为有一些客户非常致力于将大部分资产在本地运行。因此，从定义上讲，如果他们打算构建AI工作负载，就必须在本地运行，这意味着他们也需要访问GPU，他们正在这样做。而其他客户则基本上是利用从云提供商租用GPU来构建自己的AI工作负载。

No, I think it's kind of a little bit of both. I think you have some customers who are very committed to running a big part of the estate on-prem. So by definition, then if they're going to build an AI workload, it has to be run on-prem, which means that they also need access to GPUs and they're doing that. And then other customers are leveraging basically renting GPUs from the cloud providers and building their own AI workloads.

pairs.en: I do think we're in the very, very early days. They're still learning, experimenting. More-and-more apps are entering production. And as I said on the prepared remarks, we have thousands of workload -- AI workloads running on MongoDB, but a very small percentage of them have demonstrated meaningful product market fit.
pairs.zh:
我确实认为我们还处于非常非常早期的阶段。他们仍在学习、实验。越来越多的应用正在进入生产阶段。正如我在准备好的发言中所说，我们有数千个工作负载——运行在MongoDB上的AI工作负载，但其中只有很小一部分展示了有意义的产品市场契合度。

I do think we're in the very, very early days. They're still learning, experimenting. More-and-more apps are entering production. And as I said on the prepared remarks, we have thousands of workload -- AI workloads running on MongoDB, but a very small percentage of them have demonstrated meaningful product market fit.

pairs.en: And so the initial traction is kind of still small. But I think as people get more sophisticated with AI, as the AI technology matures and becomes more and more useful, I think applications will -- you'll start seeing these applications take-off. I kind of chuckled that today I see more senior leaders bragging about the chips they're using versus the apps they're building. So it just tells you that we're still in the very, very early days of this big platform shift.
pairs.zh:
因此，最初的势头还是比较小。但我认为，随着人们对AI的理解越来越深入，随着AI技术不断成熟并变得日益有用，应用将会——你将开始看到这些应用起飞。我有点好笑地发现，如今更多高层领导在炫耀他们使用什么芯片，而不是他们正在构建什么应用。这恰恰说明，我们仍处于这个重大平台变革的非常非常早期的阶段。

And so the initial traction is kind of still small. But I think as people get more sophisticated with AI, as the AI technology matures and becomes more and more useful, I think applications will -- you'll start seeing these applications take-off. I kind of chuckled that today I see more senior leaders bragging about the chips they're using versus the apps they're building. So it just tells you that we're still in the very, very early days of this big platform shift.

pairs.en: Hi, thanks for taking the question. Dev, Michael, it sounds like the takeaway from the call is a greater focus on EA and on enterprise. So should we structurally rethink the EA business differently and think of this more as a healthy double-digit growth business going forward for the foreseeable future? And then if I could just ask a follow-up question separate to that. But Michael, I understand that it's still early to identify the fiscal '25 cohort of workloads, but just curious at a high-level if they look and feel like of higher quality than the fiscal '24 cohort of workflow.

pairs.en: Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

你想要承担第一个（就EA而言）吗？  
Do you want to take on the first one in terms of EA...

pairs.en: Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer
pairs.zh:
迈克尔·戈登（Michael Gordon）——首席运营官兼首席财务官

Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer

pairs.en: Yeah, I mean, I would say, I mean, we are very committed to our run anywhere strategy. And as I said, we are first investing in community where for many customers is the first way they experience MongoDB. And we want them to have the full experience of integrating search and Vector search into our core product. And so they can out of the gate really start building applications.
pairs.zh:
是的，我的意思是，我想说，我们非常致力于我们的"随处运行"战略。正如我所说，我们首先投资于社区，因为对许多客户来说，这是他们体验MongoDB的第一种方式。我们希望他们能够全面体验到将搜索和向量搜索整合到我们核心产品中的能力。这样他们就可以从一开始就真正开始构建应用程序。

Yeah, I mean, I would say, I mean, we are very committed to our run anywhere strategy. And as I said, we are first investing in community where for many customers is the first way they experience MongoDB. And we want them to have the full experience of integrating search and Vector search into our core product. And so they can out of the gate really start building applications.

pairs.en: That will then transition to building those capabilities into EA. So, we are clearly investing in the EA product. But Atlas is still a big, big part of our business and a big, big part of our growth engine and we typically launch new features on Atlas and because of the capabilities we already have, the fact it's multi-cloud makes it a very, very compelling offering for many customers.
pairs.zh:
接下来，我们将把这些能力逐步整合到EA产品中。显然，我们正在大力投资EA产品。但Atlas仍然是我们业务中非常重要的一部分，也是我们增长引擎的关键组成部分。我们通常会在Atlas上推出新功能，而基于我们已有的能力以及其多云特性，这使得它对许多客户来说是一个非常、非常有吸引力的产品。

That will then transition to building those capabilities into EA. So, we are clearly investing in the EA product. But Atlas is still a big, big part of our business and a big, big part of our growth engine and we typically launch new features on Atlas and because of the capabilities we already have, the fact it's multi-cloud makes it a very, very compelling offering for many customers.

pairs.en: Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev C. Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. And I think in terms of the workloads, I do think it's early. Just as a reminder for folks, they tend to start small, although grow quickly. I think the only other thing that I can add is, we've been pretty consistent and that we've been pleased with the new business that we've done.
pairs.zh:
是的。从工作负载的角度来看，我认为现在还为时尚早。提醒各位一下，这些工作负载通常起步规模较小，但增长迅速。我想我唯一能补充的一点是，我们一直保持一致，并且对已开展的新业务感到满意。

原文：
Yeah. And I think in terms of the workloads, I do think it's early. Just as a reminder for folks, they tend to start small, although grow quickly. I think the only other thing that I can add is, we've been pretty consistent and that we've been pleased with the new business that we've done.

pairs.en: But we need some time to let the cohorts play out as we track them. But I think like I said, we've been happy with the new business that we were winning.

But we need some time to let the cohorts play out as we track them. But I think like I said, we've been happy with the new business that we were winning.

pairs.en: Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev C. Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. So the reason we're so excited about the opportunity to go after legacy applications is that, one, it seems like there's a confluence of events happening. One is that the increasing cost and tax of supporting and managing these legacy apps are just going up enough. Second, for many customers who are in regulated industries, the regulators are calling their -- the fact that they're running on these legacy apps with systemic risk, so they can no longer kick the can down the road.
pairs.zh:
是的，我们之所以对改造遗留应用程序的机会感到如此兴奋，原因有两点：第一，各种趋势似乎正在汇聚。一方面，支持和维护这些遗留应用程序的成本和负担正在不断上升。另一方面，对于许多受监管行业的客户来说，监管机构正在指出他们运行这些遗留应用程序带来的系统性风险，因此他们无法再拖延下去了。

原文：
Yeah. So the reason we're so excited about the opportunity to go after legacy applications is that, one, it seems like there's a confluence of events happening. One is that the increasing cost and tax of supporting and managing these legacy apps are just going up enough. Second, for many customers who are in regulated industries, the regulators are calling their -- the fact that they're running on these legacy apps with systemic risk, so they can no longer kick the can down the road.

pairs.en: Third, also because they no longer can kick the can around, some vendors are going end-of-life, so they have to make a decision to migrate those applications to a more modern tech stack. Fourth, because GenAI is so predicated on data and to build a competitive advantage, you need to leverage your proprietary data, people want to access that data and be able to do so easily. And so that's another reason for them to want to modernize. And then you also have people who built those applications who are retiring or just no longer in the firm, so it just creates more and more risk for the companies.
pairs.zh:
第三，也是因为他们不能再拖延问题了，一些供应商正在停止产品生命周期，因此他们必须决定将这些应用程序迁移到更现代的技术栈。第四，因为GenAI很大程度上依赖数据，并且要建立竞争优势，你需要利用自己的专有数据，人们希望能够轻松访问这些数据。因此，这也是他们希望进行现代化的另一个原因。此外，还有那些构建这些应用程序的人正在退休或已不在公司，这给企业带来了越来越多的风险。

Third, also because they no longer can kick the can around, some vendors are going end-of-life, so they have to make a decision to migrate those applications to a more modern tech stack. Fourth, because GenAI is so predicated on data and to build a competitive advantage, you need to leverage your proprietary data, people want to access that data and be able to do so easily. And so that's another reason for them to want to modernize. And then you also have people who built those applications who are retiring or just no longer in the firm, so it just creates more and more risk for the companies.

pairs.en: Given all that, customers are incredibly interested in figuring out a way to easily and safely and securely migrate those -- off those applications. And we always could help them very easily move the data and map the schema from a relational schema to a document schema. The hardest part was essentially rewriting the application. Now with the advent of GenAI, you can now significantly reduce the time.
pairs.zh:
考虑到这一切，客户非常想找到一种简单、安全、可靠的方法来迁移这些应用——摆脱它们。我们始终能够非常轻松地帮助他们迁移数据，并将模式从关系型模式映射到文档型模式。最困难的部分实际上是对应用程序进行重写。现在随着GenAI的出现，你可以显著缩短这一时间。

原文：
Given all that, customers are incredibly interested in figuring out a way to easily and safely and securely migrate those -- off those applications. And we always could help them very easily move the data and map the schema from a relational schema to a document schema. The hardest part was essentially rewriting the application. Now with the advent of GenAI, you can now significantly reduce the time.

pairs.en: One, you can use GenAI to analyze existing code. Two, you can use GenAI to reverse engineer tests to test what the code does. And then three, you can use GenAI to build new code and then users test it to ensure that the new code produces the same results as the old code. And so all that time and effort is suddenly cut in a meaningful way and that's suddenly creating a lot of interest from customers saying, my goodness.
pairs.zh:
第一，你可以使用GenAI分析现有代码。第二，你可以利用GenAI反向推导测试，以验证代码的功能。第三，你可以借助GenAI构建新代码，然后由用户测试以确保新代码产生与旧代码相同的结果。这样一来，所有的时间和精力都显著节省了，这瞬间引发了客户极大的兴趣，他们会惊叹道：“天哪。”

One, you can use GenAI to analyze existing code. Two, you can use GenAI to reverse engineer tests to test what the code does. And then three, you can use GenAI to build new code and then users test it to ensure that the new code produces the same results as the old code. And so all that time and effort is suddenly cut in a meaningful way and that's suddenly creating a lot of interest from customers saying, my goodness.

pairs.en: And if you're already on a relational app, moving to another relational app doesn't feel like modernization. So, the other advantage is that moving to MongoDB gives them a much more modern platform, a much more agile, flexible, performant, and scalable platform for their future needs. And that's why we're so excited. Again, it's early days.
pairs.zh:
如果你已经在使用关系型应用，那么迁移到另一个关系型应用并不会让人感觉是在现代化。所以，另一个优势是，迁移到MongoDB为他们提供了一个更现代化的平台，一个更敏捷、更灵活、性能更强、可扩展性更好的平台，以满足未来的需求。这就是我们如此兴奋的原因。不过，现在还为时过早。

And if you're already on a relational app, moving to another relational app doesn't feel like modernization. So, the other advantage is that moving to MongoDB gives them a much more modern platform, a much more agile, flexible, performant, and scalable platform for their future needs. And that's why we're so excited. Again, it's early days.

pairs.en: We've run a number of pilots that have gone well. We're in the process of working with some customers now in the migration process. This will take time because these are very, very complex applications. And actually, one thing I also mentioned was that they're not just going after -- saying go after some tertiary Tier 2 or Tier 3 application, they're saying, hey, we want you to look at some of our crown jewels because these are the apps that are most painful for us.
pairs.zh:
我们进行了一些试点项目，进展顺利。目前正在与一些客户合作推进迁移流程。这需要时间，因为这些应用程序非常非常复杂。实际上，我还提到了一点，他们不仅仅是针对——比如说针对一些二级或三级应用，而是说，嘿，我们希望你们看看我们的一些核心应用，因为这些应用对我们来说最棘手。

We've run a number of pilots that have gone well. We're in the process of working with some customers now in the migration process. This will take time because these are very, very complex applications. And actually, one thing I also mentioned was that they're not just going after -- saying go after some tertiary Tier 2 or Tier 3 application, they're saying, hey, we want you to look at some of our crown jewels because these are the apps that are most painful for us.

pairs.en: So that's also very exciting. But again, this will take time, but we're very committed to this, and we think this is going to drive -- help us drive long-term -- meaningful long-term growth.
pairs.zh:
所以这也非常令人兴奋。但同样，这需要时间，不过我们对此非常投入，我们认为这将推动——帮助我们推动长期——有意义的长期增长。

So that's also very exciting. But again, this will take time, but we're very committed to this, and we think this is going to drive -- help us drive long-term -- meaningful long-term growth.

pairs.en: Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer
pairs.zh:
迈克尔·戈登（Michael Gordon）——首席运营官兼首席财务官

Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer

pairs.en: Yeah, Will. And to the last part of your question on the professional services investment, we're really building out that capacity in order to meet the demand that we're seeing relative to the opportunity. We're calling it in particular because it has a gross margin impact because that's where that will typically show up. And then maybe the last thing and it's probably obvious, but just to sort of underscore it is the reason we're doing this though is for the ARR, right, to drive the new workloads, the additional workloads over to MongoDB as part of that migration.
pairs.zh:
是的，Will。关于你问题中最后一部分——专业服务投资，我们确实是在建设这方面的能力，以满足我们看到的与机遇相关的需求。我们特别提到这一点，是因为它会影响毛利率，因为通常它会体现在那里。最后一点可能显而易见，但我想强调一下，我们这样做的原因是为了ARR，对吧，为了推动新的工作负载，将额外的工作负载迁移到MongoDB，作为该迁移过程的一部分。

原文：
Yeah, Will. And to the last part of your question on the professional services investment, we're really building out that capacity in order to meet the demand that we're seeing relative to the opportunity. We're calling it in particular because it has a gross margin impact because that's where that will typically show up. And then maybe the last thing and it's probably obvious, but just to sort of underscore it is the reason we're doing this though is for the ARR, right, to drive the new workloads, the additional workloads over to MongoDB as part of that migration.

pairs.en: And over time, as we've talked about before, we hope and expect to be able to leverage technology more and more, but at least initially and into the medium-term, there's going to be a healthy human/services component to that. Just wanted to sort of effectively telegraph that out to folks.
pairs.zh:
随着时间的推移，正如我们之前讨论过的，我们希望并期待能够越来越多地利用技术，但至少在初期到中期，其中会包含相当大比例的人工/服务成分。只是想有效地向大家传达这一点。

And over time, as we've talked about before, we hope and expect to be able to leverage technology more and more, but at least initially and into the medium-term, there's going to be a healthy human/services component to that. Just wanted to sort of effectively telegraph that out to folks.

pairs.en: Hey guys, thanks for squeezing me in here. I believe last quarter you said consumption growth slightly ahead of expectations. And while down slower year-over-year growth versus Q2 last year, the year-over-year growth did improve from Q1. And so, I guess I'm curious for Q3, could you make a comment in that same regard? Obviously, slower on a year-over-year basis than Q3 last year, but was it stable with year-over-year consumption growth in Q2 or better or worse?
pairs.zh:
嘿，各位，感谢你们抽时间见我。我记得上季度你们提到消费增长略高于预期。虽然同比增速比去年第二季度有所放缓，但相比第一季度确实有所改善。所以，我想了解一下第三季度的情况，你们能否就同一指标做个说明？显然，第三季度同比增速会低于去年第三季度，但和第二季度的消费同比增速相比，是保持稳定、更好还是更差？

原文：Hey guys, thanks for squeezing me in here. I believe last quarter you said consumption growth slightly ahead of expectations. And while down slower year-over-year growth versus Q2 last year, the year-over-year growth did improve from Q1. And so, I guess I'm curious for Q3, could you make a comment in that same regard? Obviously, slower on a year-over-year basis than Q3 last year, but was it stable with year-over-year consumption growth in Q2 or better or worse?

pairs.en: Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer
pairs.zh:
迈克尔·戈登（Michael Gordon）——首席运营官兼首席财务官

Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. Rudy, thanks for the question. We haven't specifically called that out relative to Q2. We did see a lower year-over-year growth as we called out.
pairs.zh:
是的，Rudy，谢谢你的提问。我们并未特别针对Q2提及这一点。正如我们之前提到的，确实看到了较低的同比增长。

Yeah. Rudy, thanks for the question. We haven't specifically called that out relative to Q2. We did see a lower year-over-year growth as we called out.

pairs.en: We did see a seasonal rebound. Usually Q3 is stronger than Q2 and we talked about how that was smaller than in the prior year. So hopefully, that will help you all triangulate.
pairs.zh:
我们确实看到了季节性反弹。通常第三季度强于第二季度，我们之前讨论过这一增长幅度低于上年同期。希望这能帮助各位进行三角验证。

pairs.en: Okay. And then just a quick follow-up. I believe it was on your Q4 call back in March. At that point, Dev, you said it would be at least another year until AI applications are being deployed at scale.
pairs.zh:
好的。然后是一个快速追问。我相信这是在3月份的Q4电话会议上。当时，Dev，你说至少还需要一年时间，AI应用才能大规模部署。

Okay. And then just a quick follow-up. I believe it was on your Q4 call back in March. At that point, Dev, you said it would be at least another year until AI applications are being deployed at scale.

pairs.en: It sounds like the commentary that some early large workloads, but out of the thousands, just not many that are at large scale. I guess, is your expectation now that maybe it's still at least another year until we're seeing broad AI application rollouts at scale?
pairs.zh:
听起来像是评论说有一些早期的大型工作负载，但在数千个之中，真正达到大规模的不多。我想，您现在的预期是不是至少还要再过一年，我们才能看到大规模的AI应用部署？

It sounds like the commentary that some early large workloads, but out of the thousands, just not many that are at large scale. I guess, is your expectation now that maybe it's still at least another year until we're seeing broad AI application rollouts at scale?

pairs.en: Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev C. Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. I think a lot of it's a function of the what's happening in the R&D side of AI, right? So for example, today, we don't have a very compelling model designed for our phones, right, because today the phones don't have the computing horsepower to run complex models. So, you don't see a ton of very, very successful consumer apps besides, say, ChatGPT or Claude. So we don't -- we also don't see like hundreds of apps taking off like you saw kind of the first generation of like the Internet or the cloud era, right, or the mobile era.
pairs.zh:
是的，我认为这在很大程度上取决于AI研发领域的情况，对吧？例如，目前我们还没有为手机设计出非常强大的模型，因为现在的手机计算能力不足以运行复杂模型。所以，除了像ChatGPT或Claude这样的产品，你很难看到大量非常成功的消费者应用。我们也没有看到像第一代互联网、云时代或移动时代那样，数以百计的应用迅速崛起。

原文：
Yeah. I think a lot of it's a function of the what's happening in the R&D side of AI, right? So for example, today, we don't have a very compelling model designed for our phones, right, because today the phones don't have the computing horsepower to run complex models. So, you don't see a ton of very, very successful consumer apps besides, say, ChatGPT or Claude. So we don't -- we also don't see like hundreds of apps taking off like you saw kind of the first generation of like the Internet or the cloud era, right, or the mobile era.

pairs.en: So like I think we're still in the early days of AI. And so while we see a lot of people building AI apps, a lot of them have kind of fairly rudimentary functionality. But I think that over time that's going to change. In fact, I know it will change.
pairs.zh:
所以我认为我们仍处于AI的早期阶段。虽然我们看到很多人正在构建AI应用，但其中很多功能还相当初级。但我认为随着时间推移，这种情况将会改变。事实上，我知道它一定会改变。

So like I think we're still in the early days of AI. And so while we see a lot of people building AI apps, a lot of them have kind of fairly rudimentary functionality. But I think that over time that's going to change. In fact, I know it will change.

pairs.en: I just can't predict when that will happen. But where we do see apps having production, we are having traction, we're seeing them grow very, very quickly and we have a lot of them on our platform. It's just very few of them are really have meaningful.
pairs.zh:
我无法预测那具体何时会发生。但确实，在我们看到有应用进入生产阶段时，就有了牵引力，我们看到它们增长非常非常快，而且我们平台上有很多这样的应用。只是其中真正有意义的还很少。

I just can't predict when that will happen. But where we do see apps having production, we are having traction, we're seeing them grow very, very quickly and we have a lot of them on our platform. It's just very few of them are really have meaningful.

pairs.en: Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev C. Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev C. Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Thank you, everyone. I just wanted to say we're really pleased with our Q3 results with strong new business performance and revenue exceeding expectations both -- across both Atlas and EA. We're making the necessary investments to expand our enterprise channel where we see the largest opportunity to establish MongoDB as a standard and the strongest returns on our go-to-market investments. Looking ahead, we are encouraged by the progress we're making on both accelerating legacy app modernization with AI as well as establishing ourselves as a standard of the emerging AI tech stack for greenfield AI applications.
pairs.zh:
谢谢大家。我想说的是，我们对第三季度的业绩非常满意，新业务表现强劲，收入超出预期，在Atlas和EA两个业务上都是如此。我们正在进行必要的投资以扩大企业渠道，我们认为这是将MongoDB确立为标准的最大机会，也是我们市场推广投资回报最高的领域。展望未来，我们对于在利用AI加速传统应用现代化以及将自身确立为新兴AI技术栈标准（用于绿地AI应用）方面取得的进展感到鼓舞。

原文：Thank you, everyone. I just wanted to say we're really pleased with our Q3 results with strong new business performance and revenue exceeding expectations both -- across both Atlas and EA. We're making the necessary investments to expand our enterprise channel where we see the largest opportunity to establish MongoDB as a standard and the strongest returns on our go-to-market investments. Looking ahead, we are encouraged by the progress we're making on both accelerating legacy app modernization with AI as well as establishing ourselves as a standard of the emerging AI tech stack for greenfield AI applications.

pairs.en: And last but not least, I would like to thank Michael again for his contributions over the past 10 years and wish him well. Thank you, everyone, and we'll talk to you soon.
pairs.zh:
最后但同样重要的是，我要再次感谢Michael过去10年来的贡献，并祝他一切顺利。感谢大家，我们很快再会。

And last but not least, I would like to thank Michael again for his contributions over the past 10 years and wish him well. Thank you, everyone, and we'll talk to you soon.
